sources.summary: The paper describes M51 as a massive grand-design spiral interacting with an early-type companion, with high gas surface density, molecular fraction, star formation, and bright spiral-arm CO regions.

quotes.quote: M51 (NGC 5194) and M74 (NGC 628) are both grand design spiral galaxies within 10 Mpc distance. M51, the more massive of the two, is currently undergoing an interaction with its early type companion. It shows a higher surface density of gas in its disk, a higher molecular fraction, and more active star formation than M74.
The high I regions in M51 are the inner parts of the spiral arms.
The large spiral galaxies show higher , with average surface densities of 160 M _ pc ^ 2 in M51 and 50 M _ pc ^ 2 in M74.

sources.summary: The paper maps M51 outer-arm GMAs in dense-gas tracers, finding bright HCN and HCO+ emission, similar dense-gas ratios to other disks, and IR/dense-gas relations consistent with global trends.

quotes.quote: In order to explore the SFR--dense gas link at high resolution ( 4 , 150 pc) in the outer disk of an external galaxy, we have mapped a region about 5 kpc from the center along the northern spiral arm of M51 in the HCN(1--0), HCO ^+ (1--0) and HNC(1--0) emission lines using the Northern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA) interferometer. The HCN and HCO ^+ lines were detected in 6 giant molecular associations (GMAs) while HNC emission was only detected in the two brightest GMAs. One of the GMAs hosts a powerful H 2 region and HCN is stronger than HCO ^+ there. Comparing with observations of GMAs in the disks of M31 and M33 at similar angular resolution ( 100 pc), we find that GMAs in the outer disk of M51 are brighter in both HCN and HCO ^+ lines by a factor of 3 on average. However, the I_ HCN /I_ CO and I_ HCO^+ /I_ CO ratios are similar to the ratios in nearby galactic disks and the Galactic plane.

sources.summary: The paper uses M51 as a spiral-galaxy example where interarm gas has inefficient cloud-collision cooling, while regular arm wiggles are attributed to cloud collisions.

quotes.quote: An example of this can be found in the M51 galaxy 2008MNRAS.391..844D , see also our Fig. fig:3 .

For gas in interarm region of the Milky Way and M51, D < 1/3 (which roughly corresponds to _ disc < 1/3 _ cloud ), cloud collision cooling is inefficient (where, by definition, the time for a cloud to collide with another cloud is longer than the disc dynamical time). The gas clouds coagulate slowly, and at the same time they are sheared apart by the disc differential rotation. This slow coagulation process can produce clouds characterized by power-law mass distributions where small clouds dominate in number 1983ApJ...271..604K We note, however, that this can also be produced by theory assuming turbulence fragmentation e.g. 2013MNRAS.430.1653H .

It is believed that wiggle-like regular-spaced structures seem on the arms of the grand-design spiral galaxy M51 are produced in this way 2008MNRAS.391..844D We note that there are alternative explanation to these wiggles, where they come from the wiggle (Kelvin-Helmholtz) instability 2004MNRAS.349..270W,2006MNRAS.367..873D,2006ApJ...647..997S,2006ApJ...646..213K .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5194/95 as an interacting companion system whose star formation is linked to gas compression, with many radio-continuum data requiring careful selection.

quotes.quote: In the present sample, NGC 4490/85, NGC 5194/95 (M 51), and NGC 4631 (with nearby dwarf elliptical NGC 4627 and the more massive disk galaxy NGC 4652) have nearby and obviously interacting companion galaxies. These interactions are the likely cause of the intense ongoing star formation in these galaxies, with the close companions stirring up the gas and giving rise to gas compression and subsequent star formation out of the molecular gas.

M 51 has been the target of numerous radio continuum studies, which in the early days aimed at measuring the total radio continuum spectrum and studying cosmic-ray propagation klein84b , and later on focused on investigating the structure of the large-scale magnetic field in it e.g. 1992A&A...263...30N, 2011MNRAS.412.2396F, 2015ApJ...800...92M . The rich set of flux-density measurements available for this galaxy required a careful selection, with only the most reliable data in terms of confusion and missing short spacings culled by us. klein84b inferred a simple power-law for the synchrotron spectrum of M 51.

sources.summary: The paper uses M51 as its fiducial spiral with an extended HI disk, adopting its simulated outer-disk HI mass, star-formation rate, and metallicity to estimate binary black-hole merger rates.

quotes.quote: C11's simulations are not cosmological, but by design match the detailed morphology of M51 (and also NGC 1512 that was also studied in that paper), and the gas disk in C11 extends out to large radii ( 50 kpc ). C11 used the Gadget-2 SPH code to carry out detailed controlled simulations of M51 and NGC 1512 and fit to the observed morphology of the outer HI disk to characterize the galactic satellites in these systems. We consider C11's M51 simulation as our fiducial case here.
We begin by considering M51 as a prototypical spiral with an extended HI disk, and we adopt parameters from the simulation of M51 performed by C11 as our fiducial numbers.
From C11, we adopt M _ ,M51 = 0.1 M_ / yr and Z = Z_ M51 = 0.1 Z_ for the star formation rate and metallicity of M51 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reviews M51 dense-gas observations, saying its outer disk follows the IR-HCN relation while nuclear HCN is enhanced relative to infrared emission.

quotes.quote: Observations of dense-gas tracers toward the outer disks of galaxies that are more quiescent and relatively weaker in gas emission are still scarce. chen15 presented a \ map of M51 that covers a 4 5 region. They found that the outer disk regions of M51 on a kpc-scale follow the IR-HCN relation established globally in galaxies within the scatter and these regions bridge the luminosity gap between GMCs and galaxies. Maps of M51 in HCN, HCO ^+ , and HNC \ emission were also shown in bigiel16 . Both studies show that \ is enhanced with respect to the IR emission in the nuclear region of M51 compared to the outer disk.

It has been suggested that the enhancement of the HCN abundance at the nucleus of M51 could be attributed to the shock produced by the interaction between AGN jets and molecular gas matsushita15 . \ observations in several off-nuclear positions of nearby galaxies by usero15 show a systematic variation of the SFR per unit dense-gas mass with both the H _2 and stellar mass surface densities, which they argue is more consistent with models of turbulence-regulated star formation than with density threshold models.

sources.summary: The paper reports SOFIA HAWC+ FIR polarimetry of M51, finding spiral-aligned magnetic vectors, low central fractional polarization, and turbulent central lines of sight.

quotes.quote: SOFIA HAWC+ polarimetry at 154 is reported for the face-on galaxy M51 and the edge-on galaxy NGC 891. For M51, the polarization vectors generally follow the spiral pattern defined by the molecular gas distribution, the far-infrared (FIR) intensity contours, and other tracers of star formation. The fractional polarization is much lower in the FIR-bright central regions than in the outer regions, and we rule out loss of grain alignment and variations in magnetic field strength as causes. When compared with existing synchrotron observations, which sample different regions with different weighting, we find the net position angles are strongly correlated, the fractional polarizations are moderately correlated, but the polarized intensities are uncorrelated. We argue that the low fractional polarization in the central regions must be due to significant numbers of highly turbulent segments across the beam and along lines of sight in the beam in the central 3 kpc of M51.

sources.summary: M51 is treated with adopted distance, geometry, metallicity gradient, PAWS CO data over the inner disk, and high-resolution 24-micron imaging.

quotes.quote: M51. We adopt a distance of 8.6 Mpc bradley09 , an inclination of 21.0^ , and a position angle of 173.0^ colombo14 . The adopted metallicity at the galactic centre is 12+log(O/H)_ 0 =8.88 0.053 , with a radial gradient of 0.0223 0.0037 \ dex kpc ^ 1 , as measured by pilyugin14 using the strong-line ``counterpart'' method pilyugin12 . We use the data of the inner 10 6 kpc ^2 of the M51 presented in pety13 as part of the PdBI Arcsecond Whirlpool Survey (PAWS; schinnerer13 ). The surveyed region is visible in Figure fig:map_rgb . The PdBI observations were carried out using A, B, C, and D configurations.

sources.summary: The paper discusses M51 through PAWS, describing high-resolution CO mapping, a striking molecular-gas contrast, and links between cloud-scale ISM structure and local star formation.

quotes.quote: Subsequently, the PdBI Arcsecond Whirlpool Survey PAWS; SCHINNERER13,PETY13 mapped M51 at 1 40 pc resolution improving on a similar effort at 3 resolution using CARMA by KODA09 . At this high resolution, the contrast between the molecular gas in M51 and that of Local Group galaxies proved striking e.g., HUGHES13A, HUGHES13B . Figure fig:varyingclouds illustrates a similar contrast.

Analysis of the PAWS data helped establish that the cloud-scale structure of the molecular ISM depends on dynamical environment and host galaxy properties HUGHES13A, HUGHES13B, COLOMBO14B, LEROY16 and showed that the local star formation activity in M51 depends on cloud-scale ISM structure MEIDT13, LEROY17A . PAWS studies also demonstrated how high resolution imaging yields insight into the evolution and timescales of individual star-forming regions SCHINNERER13,MEIDT15,SCHINNERER17 .

sources.summary: The paper describes M51 as the Whirlpool galaxy, an interacting face-on spiral with a low-luminosity Seyfert 2 nucleus, symmetric star-forming arms, and a rich X-ray-source population.

quotes.quote: The galaxy M51 (NGC 5194), also known as the Whirlpool galaxy, and its companion NGC 5195 are a pair of interacting galaxies located within the constellation Canes Venatici. The face-on spiral galaxy M51 has a low luminosity Seyfert 2 nucleus (at a distance of 9 Mpc; ). The remarkable aspect of the spiral arms of M51 is that they are very symmetric and serve as star formation factories as a result of the continuous and extremely regular dust lanes and the presence of diffuse interstellar medium 1997ApJ...486L..95B . As such, M51 is a good target for studying discrete X-ray populations, including ULXs. X-ray sources in M51 were investigated using the ROSAT /HRI observations.

sources.summary: The paper studies the nearby interacting M51 pair, describing NGC 5194 as a face-on grand-design spiral and cataloging hundreds of Chandra X-ray sources and optical counterparts.

quotes.quote: We used archival data from the Chandra X-ray Observatory ( ) and the Hubble Space Telescope , to identify 334 candidate X-ray binary (XRB) systems and their potential optical counterparts in the interacting galaxy pair NGC 5194/5195 (M51).

The Whirlpool Galaxy (NGC 5194, M51) and its companion (NGC 5195) are a nearby interacting galaxy pair at a distance of 8.58 0.1 Mpc McQuinn2016 in the constellation Canes Venatici. NGC 5194 is a face-on grand design spiral galaxy that lends itself well to studies of its spiral arms, globular clusters, and X-ray binaries XRBs. There have been a large number of studies of the X-ray sources in M51 going back decades.
In this study we presented a catalog and statistical analysis of archival \ and \ data of point sources in the interacting galaxy pair NGC 5194/5195 (M51).

sources.summary: The paper presents the CLAWS atlas of CO isotopologues across M51, mapping disk-scale molecular lines and interpreting radial and environmental line-ratio variations.

quotes.quote: We present the CO isotopologue Line Atlas within the Whirpool galaxy Survey (CLAWS) based on an IRAM 30-m large programme which provides a benchmark study of numerous, faint CO isotopologues in the mm-wavelength regime across the full disc of the nearby grand-design spiral galaxy M51 (NGC 5194). The survey's core goal is to use the low- J CO isotopologue lines to constrain CO excitation and chemistry, and therefrom the local physical conditions of the gas. In this survey paper, we describe the CLAWS observing and data reduction strategies. We map the J=1 0 and 2 1 transitions of the CO isotopologues ^ 12 CO , ^ 13 CO , C^ 18 O and C^ 17 O , as well as several supplementary lines within the 1 mm and 3 mm window ( CN 10 , CS 21 , CH_3OH 21 , N_2H^+ 10 , HC_3N 109 ) at 1 kpc resolution.

We use these data to explore several CO isotopologue line ratios in detail, study their radial (and azimuthal) trends and investigate whether changes in line ratios stem from changes in ISM properties such as gas temperatures, densities or chemical abundances.

sources.summary: The paper uses Messier 51 as an X-ray image-enhancement case study, describing it as an interacting spiral with an AGN and comparing recovered point sources and extended features to Chandra contours.

quotes.quote: Messier 51 (M 51) is an interacting spiral galaxy with an active galactic nuclei, and it is another useful case study to see how the network performs ( fig:m51_contour ). In this example we see that the generated image has point sources that are better defined compared to the real image. For example, the faint source at the bottom left of the centre is clearly visible in the SR and Chandra image but are barely in the real image. Looking at the real image and generated SR with in white the contours of Chandra overlayed. We can also see that in the top left of the SR image an extended feature is visible that matches with the contours of Chandra , this extended feature is harder to be seen in the real image.

sources.summary: The paper describes M51 as a nearby spiral with a low-luminosity Seyfert 2 nucleus whose Chandra image and spectra reveal obscured nuclear emission and shock-heated extranuclear outflow clouds.

quotes.quote: We present a ACIS-S observation of the nuclear region of the nearby spiral galaxy M51 (NGC 5194), which has a low-luminosity Seyfert 2 nucleus. The X-ray image shows the nucleus, southern extranuclear cloud, and northern loop, the morphology of the extended emission being very similar to those seen in radio continuum and optical emission line images. The X-ray spectrum of the nucleus is well represented by a model consisting of soft thermal plasma with kT 0.5 keV, a very hard continuum, and an Fe K emission line at 6.45 keV with an equivalent width of > 2 keV. The very strong Fe line and the flat continuum indicate that the nucleus is obscured by a column density in excess of 10^ 24 and the spectrum is dominated by reflected emission from cold matter near the nucleus. The X-ray spectra of the extranuclear clouds are well fitted by a thermal plasma model with kT 0.5 keV.

sources.summary: The review describes M 51 as a grand-design spiral with strong total fields in its spiral arms, strong regular fields at inner arm edges, and interarm fields shaped by density waves.

quotes.quote: The mean equipartition strength of the total field for a sample of 74 spiral galaxies (Niklas 1995) is B_ t 9 G. Radio-faint galaxies like M 31 and M 33 have B_ t 6 G, while 15 G is typical for grand-design galaxies like M 51, M 83 and NGC 6946. In the prominent spiral arms of M 51 the total field strength is 30--35 G (Fletcher et al., this volume).

Exceptionally strong regular fields are detected in the interarm region of NGC 6946 of up to 13 G (Beck Hoernes 1996, see Fig. 3)) and 15 G at the inner edge of the inner spiral arms in M 51 (Fletcher, this volume).

In galaxies with strong density waves (like M 51) the regular field fills the whole interarm space, but is strongest at the positions of the dust lanes on the inner edge of the spiral arms (Fletcher, this volume).

sources.summary: The paper describes M51 as having mostly blue, metal-poor and relatively extended globular clusters, lacking the expected metal-rich bulge cluster population despite evidence for a bulge.

quotes.quote: The GC system in M51 meanwhile, appears almost exclusively blue and metal poor. This lack of metal-rich GCs associated with the M51 bulge indicates that the bulge formation history of this Sbc galaxy may have differed significantly from that of our own.
The M51 cluster population has mean (foreground reddening corrected) (B-V) and (V-I) colors of 0.67 and 0.95, with standard deviations of 0.14 and 0.17 respectively.

M51 in particular, which is similar in type to the Milky Way, appears to be lacking a metal-rich bulge GC population, and metal-rich GCs in general, despite some evidence that a central bulge does exist. This galaxy is consistent with a scenario of bulge formation through secular processes.

sources.summary: The paper uses M 51 to illustrate how planetary nebulae reveal low-surface-brightness interaction features, including a western tidal tail and a southern spiral-arm extension.

quotes.quote: On the left is a standard broad-band image of the famous interacting galaxy pair M 51. Although there are numerous signs of the interaction at high surface brightnesses, there are many lower surface-brightness features that are barely visible in this representation. However, the distribution of PN found in M51 displayed on the right give additional information on this interacting system. There is a clear tidal tail structure to the west of the secondary galaxy, and there are also signs of an extension of the spiral arm to the south. The lack of PN in the central regions of the galaxies are due to crowding effects.

sources.summary: The paper uses M 51 to illustrate revised equipartition estimates, arguing that spectral steepening and synchrotron losses make classical field strengths too high or interarm values underestimated.

quotes.quote: Pohl ( ) argued that in the spiral galaxy M 51 the spectral index of the total radio emission (integrated over the whole galaxy) is 0.5 ( _0 2.0 ) at low frequencies, but a frequency break at about 1.4 GHz due to energy losses causes a steepening of almost the whole observable radio spectrum. As a result, the classical minimum-energy field strength in the spiral galaxy M 51 is too high. However, the compilation of all data by Niklas ( ) does not indicate a significant steepening of the radio spectrum of M 51 until 25 GHz, but a flattening due to thermal emission.

In galaxies with strong magnetic fields like M 51, synchrotron loss may dominate already at a few 100 pc from the acceleration sites, e.g. in interarm regions, as indicated by spectral steepening between the spiral arms (Fletcher et al. \ ). Hence, the interarm field strengths given by Fletcher et al. ( ) are underestimates.

sources.summary: The paper says M 51 has strong total and regular magnetic fields, with field orientations following spiral-arm pitch angles and regular fields filling interarm space in its density-wave spiral pattern.

quotes.quote: Radio-faint galaxies like M 31 and M 33 have B_ t 6 G, while 15 G is typical for grand-design galaxies like M 51, M 83 and NGC 6946. In the prominent spiral arms of M 51 the total field strength is 30 G (Fletcher et al. \ ), with peaks along the prominent dust lane (Fig. m51 ).
Exceptionally strong regular fields are detected in the interarm regions of NGC 6946 of up to 13 G (Beck Hoernes \ ) and 15 G at the inner edge of the inner spiral arms in M 51 (Fletcher et al. \ ).

In M 51, the field orientation varies by 10 ^ -20 ^ around the pitch angles of the spiral arms as traced by CO emission (Patrikeev et al. \ ). In galaxies with strong density waves, like M 51, the regular field fills the whole interarm space, but is strongest at the positions of the dust lanes on the inner edge of the spiral arms (Fletcher et al. \ , Fig. m51 ).

sources.summary: The paper applies its GMC-encounter disruption model to M51, using the galaxy's central molecular gas density to predict short star-cluster disruption times consistent with observations.

quotes.quote: Assuming that h is the same in M51, and therefore = /200 pc, we can predict t_4 due to GMCs encounters once we know in M51 and compare this to the value of t_4 for clusters in M51 2005A&A...441..949G . 1993A&A...274..123G find =90 pc ^ 2 for the central region of M51, which is 14 times higher than in the solar neighbourhood 1987ApJ...319..730S . This implies t_4 2100 Myr /14=150 Myr.

The resulting value of t_4 , however, is very close to the value of t_4=100-200 Myr, determined observationally by 2005A&A...441..949G from the age and mass distribution of star clusters in M51. Based on this work we think that we can explain the short disruption times of clusters in the centre of M51, if GMC encounters are the dominant disruption effect.

sources.summary: The paper uses M51 as a grand-design spiral case for cluster disruption, noting short observed cluster lifetimes and concluding spiral-arm perturbations cannot explain them.

quotes.quote: The observed short disruption time ( 100 Myr) of disc clusters in the grand-design spiral galaxy M51 2005A&A...441..949G is an order of magnitude lower than expected from the tidal field of the galaxy 2005A&A...429..173L .

Stellar arms Disc clusters are found to have short disruption times both in our Galaxy and in M51. Therefore, we will hereafter consider two fiducial spiral galaxies, one Milky-Way-like -- which we will call for brevity Milky Way, or MW -- and the other M51-like -- which we will call grand design spiral.

For M51 the difference is almost three orders of magnitude. This suggest that spiral arm perturbations contribute little to the disruption of the (low mass) open clusters in the solar neighbourhood and can definitely not explain the observed short disruption time of clusters in M51 2005A&A...441..949G .

sources.summary: The paper treats M51 as a nearby face-on grand-design spiral and uses gas, stellar, HI, radio-continuum, and ACS data to map disk stability and star formation conditions.

quotes.quote: M51, as a nearby face-on galaxy grand design spiral studied in many line transitions, is an ideal target to study the connection between physical conditions of the gas and star formation activity. In this contribution we combine molecular, atomic, total gas and stellar surface densities and study the gravitational stability of the gas (Schuster et al.2007, Hitschfeld et al. in prep.). From our IRAM-30m ^ 12 CO2-1 map and complementary HI-, Radio Continuum- and ACS B-band-data we derive maps of the total gas density and the stellar surface density to study the gravitational stability of the gas via the Toomre Q parameter.
The inner spiral arm structure of M51 in the northern part is much less prominent than in the integrated intensity map (Schuster et al. 2007).

sources.summary: M 51 is grouped with NGC 6946 as a luminous spiral lying well above the Local Sheet in an uncrowded region, unlikely to be a dwarf masquerading as a large galaxy.

quotes.quote: Among the ten most luminous galaxies in Figure 1 the spirals M 51, M 101, and NGC 6946 are 2.4, 2.8, and 4.0 Mpc above the center plane of the Local Sheet. They are in an uncrowded region: of the 562 known galaxies at 1<D<8 Mpc just 5.0 are more than 2 Mpc above the plane of the Local Sheet (while 73 of the known galaxies are within 2 Mpc of the plane and the rest are below the plane). But 30 of the largest galaxies are more than 2 Mpc above the Local Sheet.

These three could not be dwarfs masquerading as large galaxies; their circular velocities indicate the central masses of large galaxies. That is, the presence of these three large galaxies in the uncrowded region above the Local Sheet is real, and at well below 1 probability it is unlikely within standard ideas.

sources.summary: The review uses M51 as an S4G imaging example, emphasizing its outer tidal structure from interaction and a revised dust-free infrared morphological classification.

quotes.quote: As an example of the data quality, including depth of imaging and field of view related to the size of the galaxy, that will be delivered by the S ^4 G for all survey galaxies, we present in Fig. s4gfig a 3.6 m image of the well-known galaxy M51. The image is reproduced from a paper by Buta et al. (2010b), which discusses the morphology of the first 207 S ^4 G galaxies. Not only does the image show the intricate outer tidal structure caused by the interaction of M51 and its companion NGC 5194 (see Salo Laurikainen 2000), it also illustrates how a high-quality dust-free vision of a galaxy can lead to a better insight into its nature. On the basis of this new image, Buta et al. (2010b) were able to revise the morphological classification of M51 from SA(s)bc pec (RC3) to S A B(rs,nr)bc, but much more spectacular is the revision of the type of the companion of M51, NGC 5195, which was classified as an I0 pec galaxy in de Vaucouleurs et al. (1991), but which Buta et al. (2010b) could classify as SAB()0/a pec.

sources.summary: The paper uses M51 as a nearby spiral analogue, describing its cloud and massive-star distributions, overlapping cluster populations, and supernova-enriched background in its spiral-arm environment.

quotes.quote: Figure  fig:m51 plots the distribution of clouds and massive stars in the nearly face-on spiral M51 . The clusters and clouds follow the spiral arm pattern, of course, but there is clearly considerable overlap between young clusters, old clusters, and clouds as might be expected from a feedback between star and cloud formation. All the clusters shown here are large with N_* 10^3 and, assuming the same stellar mass distribution as in the Galaxy, would be expected to contain several supernova progenitors. Averaged over several Myr, this would produce a fairly constant flux of ejecta into the surrounding interstellar medium.

sources.summary: The paper says M 51 has a large-scale spiral magnetic field in its disk, visible in polarized intensity but patchy in Faraday rotation because of an additional turbulent component.

quotes.quote: Observations of M 51 showed, however, that even if a large-scale spiral magnetic field is present in the galaxy's disk and apparent in polarized intensity, it is hidden in the map of Faraday rotation fletcher10 and looks much more patchy than that in row 3 of Fig. fig:sim5ghz . This may be due to an additional magnetic field component, probably an isotropic turbulent magnetic field with scales of the longer axis between 400 pc to 1 kpc. Such a field has been found in M 51 fletcher10 and in the barred galaxy NGC 1097 beck05b , but was not included into our simulations shown in Fig. fig:sim5ghz and Fig. fig:sim150mhz . This component may be produced by strong shearing gas motions or compressions.

sources.summary: The paper uses M51 as the host of SN 2011dh, noting its recent supernova history, adopted distance and velocity, foreground reddening, and metallicity at the supernova site.

quotes.quote: The Whirlpool galaxy has now hosted three modern SNe (1994I, 2005cs and 2011dh), and the Hubble Heritage images 2005AAS...206.1307M provide deep multi-color images for a progenitor star search, as already done for SN 2005cs 2005MNRAS.364L..33M,2006ApJ...641.1060L .

We adopt a distance of 7.1 1.2 Mpc 2006MNRAS.372.1735T , and a recessional velocity of 600 from NED http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ . The foreground reddening towards M51 as quoted by NED, after schleg98 , is E(B - V ) = 0.035 . Using the metallicity gradient derived by 2004ApJ...615..228B for H ii regions in M51, we adopt a metallicity at the SN position of 0.9 0.1 Z_ .

sources.summary: The paper treats M51 as SN 2011dh’s nearby host, discusses possible M51 line-of-sight absorption, and notes its X-ray study and population of nearby ULXs.

quotes.quote: The Type IIb SN 2011dh exploded in the nearby galaxy M51 (the Whirlpool Galaxy) and provides us with one of the best laboratory to study early high energy emission from SNe.

One question is if this instrinsic absorbing column density is intrinsic to the source or due to some intervening matter in M51 along the line of sight but far away from the SN site. M51 is a nearby galaxy and it is well studied in the X--ray band. M51 hosts a number of ultra-luminous X--ray sources (ULXs) well studied and characterized (Dewangan et al. 2005; Yoshida et al. 2010).

sources.summary: The paper presents deep Burrell Schmidt imaging of M51, tracing faint tidal morphology, discovering faint South and Northeast plumes, and arguing simulations must explain the system's debris and star-poor H 1 tail.

quotes.quote: We present deep, wide-field imaging of the M51 system using CWRU's Burrell Schmidt telescope at KPNO to study the faint tidal features that constrain its interaction history. Our images trace M51's tidal morphology down to a limiting surface brightness of _ B,lim 30 mag arcsec ^ 2 , and provide accurate colors ( _ < 0.1 ) down to _B 28 . We identify two new tidal streams in the system (the South and Northeast Plumes) with surface brightnesses of _B =29 and luminosities of 10^6 L_ ,B .

Extant simulations of M51 have difficulty matching both the wealth of tidal structure in the system and the lack of stars in the H 1 tail, motivating new modeling campaigns to study the dynamical evolution of this classic interacting system.

sources.summary: The paper compares M51 spiral-arm clouds, noting that two positions have essentially the same chemistry despite different star-formation efficiency, implying weak local feedback effects.

quotes.quote: A similar result for molecular-cloud scale chemical compositions in a much larger scale (kpc scale) is recently reported for the spiral arm clouds of the external galaxy M51 watanabe2014spectral . They observed the two positions, P1 and P2, in the spiral arm of M51, which are separated by 20'' , with the IRAM 30 m telescope. Although the star formation efficiency evaluated from the H and 24 m emission is higher in P1 than in P2 by a factor of 1.5, the chemical composition is essentially the same in the two positions. Thus, local star formation and its feedback do not strongly affect the spectral pattern observed at a resolution of a molecular-cloud scale.

sources.summary: The paper describes M 51 as a detailed multifrequency magnetic-field case where disk and halo regions show different spiral magnetic modes, with LOFAR probing its outer disk.

quotes.quote: A rather more detailed application of the same basic analysis has been performed for the individual case of M 51 fletcher_etal_2011 . By drawing together a large multifrequency data set spanning wavelengths from 3 to 20 cm, the authors were able to consider separately the thermal electron population and magnetic field properties in the disk and halo regions. Ultimately they found that different spiral modes were present in the magnetic fields of the disk and halo ( m=0+2 in the disk, and m=1 in the halo; note that the qualitative appearance of the RM maps presented by heald_etal_2009 are consistent both with this model and with the interpretation of the 1 GHz emission originating from above the disk).

For example, total intensity LOFAR observations have been used to probe into the far outer disk e.g., in M51; mulcahy_etal_2014 and into the upper disk-halo interface region (e.g., NGC 5775; Heald et al. in prep, see Figure figure:lofar ).

sources.summary: The paper uses spatially resolved M51 measurements as extragalactic comparison data, noting inner and outer M51 points and lower dense-gas star formation efficiency in the inner part.

quotes.quote: The other two green points are from the study of M51 with 1 kpc resolution as presented by 2015ApJ...810..140C ; the highest green point is for the outer parts of M51, while the lower one at higher masses is from the inner 1.66 kpc.

Observations of HCN in M51 with a resolution of 1 kpc bridge the gap between the Milky Way clouds and whole galaxies. The green points are based on data in 2015ApJ...810..140C ; the higher point is the average for the outer part, while the lower point is for the inner part of M51.
This dependence can be seen in Figure galmdense where the lowest point is for the inner part of M51.

sources.summary: The paper uses PAWS observations of M51 to motivate GMC mass-function modeling, emphasizing spatially resolved CO data and regional slope variations between arms and inter-arm regions.

quotes.quote: Observed mass functions The PAWS program was conducted by using PdBI and IRAM 30 m telescope and observed the disk of the Whirlpool Galaxy M51 in ^ 12 CO(1-0) line over the 200 hours integration. The survey reveals the cold gas kinematics with the 40 pc spatial resolution and detects objects whose mass are 1.2 10^5 at the 5 level Pety2013 . The PAWS team subdivide M51 into seven regions to analyze the GMC properties in various galactic environment (see Figure 2 in Colombo2014a ). One of the highlighting results is the GMCMF variation; bar and arm regions typically have shallower slopes ( , 1.33 in the ``nuclear'' bar region and 1.75 in the ``density-wave spiral arm'' region) whereas inter-arm regions (and an arm region at M51 outskirts) typically have steeper slopes ( , 2.55 in the ``downstream'' region).

sources.summary: The paper uses M51's observed cluster population to infer a mass-dependence parameter for disruption and compare predicted median cluster ages against observations.

quotes.quote: The M51 sample is included with three different lower mass limits in order to test the dependence of equation ( eq:law ) on M_ min . M83 is divided into the inner and outer fields from Bastian12 to probe the environmental dependence in a homogeneous sample, although the inclusion of M31 already pushes the parameter space covered to extremely low gas surface densities. Before deriving the predicted median ages, we require an estimate of . In Section sec:law , we proposed that the power-law slope between the median cluster age and the minimum present-day cluster mass provides a direct measurement of . We see that the median age in M51 increases by 0.9 dex over a 1.7 dex increase in M_ min and carry out a power-law fit to the median age as a function of the minimum mass, resulting in a value of 0.54 for the cluster population in M51.

We note that this value of is consistent with the =0.57 0.10 obtained previously for M51 by assuming a constant disruption rate in time and space Lamers05b . As discussed in this paper, our measurement of =0.54 is model-independent. We use the value of =0.54 derived for M51 to predict the median cluster ages in the final column of Table tab:obs for all six cluster samples.

sources.summary: The paper finds M51 has the lowest free-fall star formation efficiency in the sample, agrees with previous M51 measurements, and suggests strong gas flows may suppress star formation.

quotes.quote: Our median 0.7 in the whole sample is about twice the 0.30-0.36 found by LEROY17 using an almost identical methodology to study M51 at 40 pc resolution. M51 is also part of our sample, and our measurements for that galaxy agree well with those in LEROY17 . This appears to reflect a real difference between M51 and the rest of our sample, i.e. M51 has the lowest \ of any galaxy in our sample. Following MEIDT13 , this may reflect strong gas flows in M51 that act to stabilize the gas and suppress star formation.

sources.summary: The paper uses the PAWS GMC catalog for M51 and identifies M51 as the only sample galaxy with a high-mass cutoff in both GMCs and young clusters.

quotes.quote: M51: A catalog of 1507 GMCs was published by Colombo14 , based on IRAM CO(1-0) observations taken as part of the PAWS survey Schinnerer13 . They assume a value for _ CO of 4.4 M _ (K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 ) ^ 1 . The survey covers the center and both inner spiral arms of the galaxy, but not the outer arms.
M51 remains the only galaxy in our sample with evidence for a high-mass cutoff in both the GMC and young cluster populations.

sources.summary: The paper cites M 51 as evidence for diffuse molecular gas: single-dish data recover more flux than interferometry, at least half its molecular gas may be diffuse or thick-disc material.

quotes.quote: pety13 studied the molecular gas in M 51 using both single-dish and interferometric observations and found that the single-dish observation recovers much more flux than the interferometric observation. They conclude that at least 50 of gas in the molecular disc of M 51 is in the diffused state, which is resolved out in the interferometric observation.
pety13 estimated that at least 50 of the molecular gas in M51 is in the thick disc.
For example, the PdBI Arcsecond Whirlpool Survey (PAWS) images the M51 galaxy with a spatial resolution of 40 pc pety13,schinnerer13 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M51 as a resolved spiral-galaxy comparison for [C ii]/infrared trends, noting an offset attributed to cooler diffuse neutral HI clouds in its interstellar medium.

quotes.quote: We now turn to discuss the [ C ii ] line deficit in spatially resolved samples, Orion A and M51. Figure 2 of shows the [ C ii ] /TIR versus TIR correlation and the [ C ii ] /TIR versus 70 m / 160 m correlation in the spiral galaxy M51 (with a spatial resolution of 16 corresponding to 660 pc ).

The M51 relationship is slightly offset from the Orion data to lower 70 m / 160 m ratios and/or lower [ C ii ] /FIR. We ascribe this to a contribution of diffuse neutral HI clouds in the interstellar medium of M51 which is characterized by cooler dust.

sources.summary: The paper measures M51's UV disk morphology, describing a non-monotonic surface-brightness distribution, negligible nuclear UV contribution, high surface brightness, and easier high-redshift detectability.

quotes.quote: Whereas M33 and M101 exhibit a rather linear profile, as expected for exponential disks, M51 and M81 have a non-monotonic distribution and M100 may be viewed as an intermediate case.
M51 is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy and M81 as an AGN galaxy but the contribution from the nucleus to the overall UV emission is not important.
M101 is a diffuse object whereas M51 has a very high surface brightness. As expected the detection of M51 at high z is far easier than that of M101 in spite of their absolute luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper uses HST cluster samples in M51 to derive cluster disruption parameters, finding a common disruption slope across the galaxy sample but the shortest t_4 in the inner region of M51.

quotes.quote: We demonstrate the method and derive the values of t_4 and from cluster samples in selected fields of four galaxies: M51, M33, SMC and the solar neighbourhood. The values of are the same within their uncertainties in the four galaxies with < >= 0.62 0.06 . However, the disruption time t_4 of clusters of 10^4 M_ is very different in the different locations. It is shortest in the inner region of M51 ( t_4 = 4 10^7 yrs) and highest in the SMC ( t_4=4 10^9 yrs).

sources.summary: The paper uses M51 to illustrate locally strong total and ordered magnetic fields in spiral arms and regular fields at the inner edge of a density-wave spiral arm.

quotes.quote: The mean equipartition value for the total magnetic field strength for a sample of 74 spiral galaxies observed by niklas95 is on average 9 3 G but reaches locally higher values within the spiral arms of up to 20 - 25 G in M51 fletcher+2011 .
The strength of the ordered magnetic fields in spiral galaxies are typically 1--5 G, and may reach locally values up to 10 - 15 G as e.g. in NGC 6946 beck07 and M51 fletcher+2011 .

\ The turbulent magnetic field is typically strongest within the optical spiral arms, whereas the regular fields are strongest in between the optical spiral arms, or at the inner edge of the density-wave spiral arm as seen in M51 fletcher+2011 (Figure m51 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses M51 as a face-on spiral comparison, citing prior work where its nuclear gas properties peak and its spiral-arm and inter-arm PDRs have similar densities and FUV radiation fields.

quotes.quote: Most recently, parkin2013 examined the n and G_0 in various regions of M51; the hydrogen density and FUV radiation peak in the nucleus and similarly decline in both the spiral arm and interarm regions, suggesting similar physical conditions in clouds in these environments (see also parkin2014 ).

In particular, we are interested in comparing the gas heating and cooling mechanisms observed in the disc from a near-perfect edge-on orientation, as in NGC 891, to those of the face-on spiral galaxy, M51.

The spiral arm and inter-arm regions in M51 both exhibit hydrogen densities and FUV radiation field strengths of 2.75 n 3 cm ^ 3 and 2.25 G_0 2.5 , respectively, despite the latter region have lower star formation rate surface densities compared to in the spiral arms, suggesting that the molecular clouds have similar properties but are more abundant in the arms than inter-arm regions parkin2013 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M51 to discuss the tight radio-CO relation, molecular gas not tied to massive star formation, and possible magnetic-field or cosmic-ray explanations.

quotes.quote: The correlation between the non-thermal radio continuum and the molecular gas (traced via its CO line emission), observed with 60 pc resolution in the spiral galaxy M51 (Schinnerer et al. 2013), is tighter than the correlation between radio and IR emission, but even less well understood. Much of the molecular gas in the spiral arms of M51 is not directly associated with ongoing massive star formation, implying that star formation cannot be the sole cause for the radio--CO relation. Two scenarios are consistent with the M51 data: (1) increased synchrotron emission from secondary CREs produced in the interaction of the CRs with the dense molecular material (e.g. Murgia et al. 2005) or (2) enhanced radio emission due to a coupling between the gas density and total magnetic field strength (e.g. Niklas Beck 1997), in connection with fast diffusion of CREs along the ordered magnetic fields of the spiral arms.

sources.summary: The paper uses M51 as a comparison spiral where ordered magnetic fields peak in dust-lane arms in some regions, are displaced elsewhere, and show radius-dependent pitch angles.

quotes.quote: Another example is provided by M51 where the ordered magnetic field is maximum at the spiral arms traced by dust lanes in some regions and displaced from them at other locations fletcher11 . When applying a wavelet analysis with anisotropic wavelets, the pitch angle of the spiral arms in M51 was found to vary with the galacto-centric radius patrikeyev06 . Systematic shifts between the spiral arm ridges in gas, dust, and magnetic field indicate a time sequence that is consistent with the gaseous spiral arms causing compression that results in a stronger magnetic field.

sources.summary: The LoTSS paper presents M 51 as a nearby grand-design spiral where low-frequency work traces old cosmic-ray electrons in an extended disk and diffusion signatures.

quotes.quote: The LoTSS images can be used to examine the propagation of cosmic ray electrons in nearby galaxies such as M 106 and M 51 (Figure fig:science-examples ). For example, and used similar observations to reveal synchrotron emission from a highly extended disk of old low-energy electrons that have propagated out to a radius of 16 kpc from the centre of the grand-design spiral galaxy M 51. A comparison of scale lengths at low and high frequencies as well as the scale-dependent radio to far infrared correlations at low and high frequencies gave clear evidence for the propagation of cosmic rays by diffusion.

sources.summary: The paper maps M51 star-formation histories and X-ray point sources to calibrate how X-ray binary luminosity functions evolve with stellar population age.

quotes.quote: We first utilize detailed stellar population synthesis modeling of far-UV to far-IR photometry of the nearby face-on spiral galaxy M51 to construct maps of the star-formation histories (SFHs) on subgalactic ( 400 pc) scales. Next, we use the 850 ks cumulative \ exposure of M51 to identify and isolate 2--7 keV detected point sources within the galaxy, and we use our SFH maps to recover the local properties of the stellar populations in which each \ source is located. We then divide the galaxy into various subregions based on their SFH properties (e.g., star-formation rate [ SFR ] per stellar mass [ M_ ] and mass-weighted stellar age) and group the \ point sources according to the characteristics of the regions in which they are found.

sources.summary: The paper describes M 51 as radio-dim across its disk but radio-bright between it and its companion; its smoothing lengthscale nearly triples, and diffusion dominates transport.

quotes.quote: The disk of M 51 is rather radio-dim, whereas the region between M 51 and its companion is radio-bright.
The smoothing lengthscales stay the same within 20 for all galaxies except NGC 6946 and M 51 where they almost triple.
Diffusion is the main transport mechanism in unperturbed galactic disks (NGC 6946, M 51, and the central region of NGC 4321).

sources.summary: The paper reports systematic monitoring of the M51 galaxies, which harbor neutron-star ULXs, transients, and many other X-ray sources targeted with long-term lightcurves.

quotes.quote: Combined with the high-quality \ lightcurve of the transient, serendipitous observations made with \ and \ provide insights into the onset and evolution of a likely super-Eddington event. stars: neutron -- galaxies: individual (M51) -- X-rays: binaries The galaxies of M51, which consist of the main spiral galaxy M51a/NGC5194 and its smaller companion M51b/NGC5195, are a host to a multitude of X-ray sources e.g. brightman18b .

Since the discoveries that M51 harbors two ULXs powered by neutron stars, we have obtained monitoring of the galaxies with \ in order to identify potential periodic flux modulations and off states exhibited by the other sources of this class.
Finally, M51 has also hosted several transient phenomena such as the type II supernova SN 2005cs, the type IIb supernova SN 2011dh and the intermediate luminosity red transient AT 2019abn jencson19 .

sources.summary: The paper presents M51, the Whirlpool Galaxy, as an 8.6 Mpc external galaxy whose X-ray-source population includes the ultraluminous supersoft source M51-ULS-1.

quotes.quote: It orbits one of the brightest XRSs in the external galaxy M51, the Whirlpool Galaxy, located 8.6 Megaparsecs from Earth.

The method can be applied to external galaxies like M51, because the field of view of the present generation of X-ray detectors is large enough to encompass dozens of bright XRSs, and the total exposure times extend to about 1 Ms ( 11.6 d).
X-Ray Properties The X-ray source exhibiting the apparent transit is M51-ULS-1, one of the brightest XRSs in M51, located at right ascension and declination 13:29:43.30, +47:11:34.7, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper cites M51 observations of a CO line ratio, reporting an average disk value and lower interarm than arm and central-region values that agree with the simulations.

quotes.quote: Vlah13 studied this ratio in the spiral galaxy M51 (NGC 5194) using the James Clerk Maxwell Telescope (JCMT). At a spatial resolution of 600 pc , they reported an average value of R_ 32 0.5 for the disk. Notably, they found R_ 32 0.2 in interarm regions and R_ 32 0.6 in arm and central regions.

sources.summary: The paper uses HAWC+ observations of M51 as a real-galaxy comparison for synthetic far-infrared polarization, finding the high-intensity model relation agrees with observed fits.

quotes.quote: While HAWC+ has indeed been able to trace the magnetic field structure in M51 and NGC 891 2020JonesHAWCFarinfraredObservations , these results probe a relatively limited part of the magnetic field geometry compared with similar extinction measurements.
We compare our predictions with HAWC+ data for M51 and NGC 891, and investigate how the synthetic polarisation signal varies as a function of the instrument resolution and wavelength range.
At the high intensity end, the relation agrees well with the least square fits to the M51 and NGC 891 data from 2020JonesHAWCFarinfraredObservations .

sources.summary: The review cites M51's nucleus as a quiescent-galaxy charge-exchange candidate, with RGS N, O, and Ne line emission possibly tied to jet-ISM interaction or past AGN photoionization.

quotes.quote: obs42 reported a possible detection of charge exchange component which contributes significantly to the N, O, and Ne lines, in particular the He-like forbidden lines, in the RGS spectrum of M51 nucleus. The interaction between the radio jet and the cold interstellar medium might be responsible for the emission. Yet another possibility is that these lines originate from photoionization by the AGN outbursts in the past.

sources.summary: The paper describes M 51 as a nearby face-on spiral where a radio-bright region toward NGC 5195 likely traces cosmic-ray advection driven by gravitational tides.

quotes.quote: The radio-bright region in M 51 (Fig. fig:rcfir_spixx1c1_nice_5 ) is located toward NGC 5195 and is thus most probably due to CR advection via gravitational tides.
NGC 4330, NGC 4402, NGC 4522, NGC 4532, and M 51 are clear cases of CR particle advection via ICM ram pressure, galactic winds, or gravitational tides (Sect. sec:radiobright ).
The correlation strength is not linked to perturbations: M 51 and NGC 4567/68 are gravitationally interacting systems with very different SFR--PI Spearman correlation coefficients.

sources.summary: The paper cites K-band images of M 51 revealing spiral arms that extend farther inward than optical images show, including an additional 540 degrees of winding.

quotes.quote: For example, Zaritsky et al. (1993) has presented K-band (2.2 m ) images of M 51, which reveal remarkable dynamical structure not visible in the conventional optical observations, and show that the spiral arms extend significantly further towards the galaxy's center than previously observed. In the optical images, the spiral arms begins at a radius of about 30^ '' from the center. But, the K-band residual image showed the spiral arms wind through an additional 540^ beyond that seen in the B-band images of the entire galaxy, and end at about 10^ '' from the center of the galaxy.

gmw_00000314.name: NGC 5195
gmw_00000314.overview: NGC 5195 is a barred lenticular companion galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0702011, arXiv:1811.11769] interacting with the spiral galaxy M51 [arXiv:0912.1201, arXiv:2009.07280]. Classified as a LINER [arXiv:2009.07280, arXiv:2212.08305], it is in a post-starburst phase [arXiv:1811.11769, arXiv:2106.05982] with suppressed massive star formation [arXiv:1811.11769] and an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1811.11769, arXiv:1805.12140].

sources.summary: NGC 5195 is modeled as M51’s companion on a bound, highly elliptical multiple-encounter orbit, represented as a one-third-mass interacting galaxy in the simulations.

quotes.quote: In order to reproduce spiral structure similar to that observed in M51, we model a galaxy representing M51 and its companion galaxy NGC 5195. We assign the initial positions and velocities of the two galaxies according to the results from N-body calculations by Theis2003 . Theis2003 used MINGA , a restricted N-body code combined with a genetic algorithm code, to determine the orbit of M51 and NGC 5195.

In their calculations, the interacting galaxy NGC 5195 is represented by a single point mass, which has one third the mass of M51. The test particles otherwise comprise the disc and bulge of M51 - the halo is represented by a potential. They generated spatial and velocity maps from the outcome of each N-body calculation to compare with observed maps of HI.

The genetic algorithm code is used to constrain the parameters of the orbit, and find the best fit to the observed data. The best-fit model corresponds to a highly elliptical orbit, with two passages of NGC 5195 through the plane of the disc of M51.

sources.summary: The paper finds that companion M51b has a [C ii] deficit, elevated dust temperature, suppressed massive star formation, and AGN activity despite bright infrared emission.

quotes.quote: The companion galaxy M51b, however, shows a deficit of [ C ii ] emission compared with the TIR emission and SFR surface density, with [ C ii ] emission detected only in the S--W part of this galaxy. The [ C ii ] deficit is associated with an enhanced dust temperature in this galaxy. We interpret the faint [ C ii ] emission in M51b to be a result of suppressed star formation in this galaxy, while the bright mid-- and far--infrared emission, which drive the TIR and SFR values, are powered by other mechanisms.

M51b is intrinsically very faint in [ C ii ] considering the observed TIR intensity and the inferred SFR. Additionally, this galaxy shows enhanced 24 m emission (Figure fig:cii_vs_tir_sfr ) and elevated dust temperature MentuchCooper2012 compared to M51. M51b is a barred lenticular galaxy in a post--starburst phase, in which the stellar population is dominated by old stars ( 10 Gyr) and massive star-formation is suppressed Kohno2002a,Alatalo2016 . The lack of massive star formation in M51b is consistent with the faint [ C ii ] emission detected but it is inconsistent with the large TIR emission observed in this galaxy and the high SFR inferred from H and 24 m dust continuum emission. M51b shows Active Galactic Nuclei (AGN) activity, based on its near--infrared line emission Goulding2009 and there is evidence of AGN feedback producing arcs and shocks in X--ray and H emission Schlegel2016,Rampadarath2018 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5195/M51b as an early-type interacting LINER/AGN host with a newly found 10 kpc ionized structure, likely from past AGN outflow or illuminated tidal debris.

quotes.quote: The early-type galaxy NGC 5195 (alternatively known as M51b) possesses extended gas features detected in multi-wavelength, postulated to be associated with previous activities of the central supermassive black hole (SMBH). Using integral field spectroscopic observations from the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope (CFHT)/SITELLE, we report on the discovery of a new large-scale ionized gas structure traced by [ O iii ] , [ N ii ] , and H line emission, extending to 10 kpc from the nucleus of NGC 5195. Its bipolar morphology, emission line ratio diagnostics, and comparison with the X-ray image from Chandra and low-frequency radio data from LOFAR all indicate that it is likely an outflow inflated by a past episode of elevated active galactic nucleus (AGN) activity. Assuming the ionized gas is outflowing from the central region of NGC 5195, the estimated mass and energy outflow rates are M _ out = 3.5 - 27.9 M_ yr^ 1 and E _ out = 0.98 - 7.9 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 , respectively, which cannot be provided by current star formation and the low luminosity nucleus.

The interacting system Messier 51 (M51) contains a grand design spiral galaxy NGC 5194 (M51a) and an early-type galaxy NGC 5195 (M51b). NGC 5195 is classified as different types of the galaxy, such as SB 0_ 1 pec 1981rsac.book.....S and I0-pec 1991rc3..book.....D , due to its peculiar morphology that is tidally disturbed in the interaction. An SMBH with mass M_ BH 10^ 7 M_ is believed to reside in the nucleus of NGC 5195 e.g. 2016ApJ...823...75S,2018MNRAS.476.2876R . According to the optical emission-line ratios, the nucleus is classified as the low ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) 1997ApJS..112..315H .

sources.summary: The paper describes M51b as the SB0 companion NGC5195, dominated by low-mass X-ray binaries, with probable high-velocity gas and a likely Northwest Plume tidal tail.

quotes.quote: M51 is a one of well known interacting systems in the local Universe, consisting of a nearly face-on spiral galaxy M51a (NGC5194), and its companion M51b (NGC5195). Since the grand-design spiral arm of M51a was discovered by Lord Rosse in 1845, many works have focused on this interacting galaxy pair in both observation and theory to study galaxy evolution, tidal tails and star formation e.g. Toomre1981, Bastian2005, Calzetti2005, Kennicutt2007 . M51 multi-wavelength images from X-ray to radio have been observed to study its morphology and kinematics e.g. Rots1990, Dale2005, Watkins2015, Heyer2022 . M51a is a Sbc galaxy with a weak Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus (AGN) in its centre, while M51b is an early-type SB0 galaxy deVaucouleurs1995 . A deep X-ray image using Chandra shows the distribution of X-ray point sources. The luminosity function suggests that M51a is dominated by high-mass X-ray binaries, while M51b is dominated by low-mass X-ray binaries Kuntz2016 . In optical bands, the magnificent spiral arms of M51a, three-pronged structure of M51b and bridge between galaxies are system's iconic structures.

We consider this gas to be the possible gas of M51b. Firstly, its location is relatively close to the M51b optical centre. The blue vertical line in Fig. fig:pv (e) represents the position of M51b's centre, which matches perfectly to the gas's edge. Second, its high velocity is substantially different from the disc velocity of M51a, indicating that it is not M51a's gas. Third, the gap shown in Fig. fig:channel_map between it and the Northwest Plume implies that it is not part of the Northwest Plume.

sources.summary: The paper describes M51b as the smaller lenticular companion of M51, linked by an interaction bridge whose diffuse emission is masked or modeled as part of faint outer emission.

quotes.quote: First discovered by Charles Messier in 1773, M51 is a system of interacting galaxies consisting of M51a (=NGC 5194), the Whirlpool Galaxy , a grand-design spiral of Hubble type Sbc, and the smaller lenticular galaxy M51b (=NGC 5195), of Hubble type SB0. While interaction between the two galaxies is evident from the bridge linking them, there is still debate of whether the galaxies have interacted on a single fly-by collision ( Toomre1972 ; Durrell2003 ) or by multiple close passes ( Salo2000 ; Dobbs2010 ). Despite the interaction, as mentioned before, M51a exhibits a good deal of azimuthal symmetry.

We also masked the companion galaxy M51b and it's diffuse emission. Convolutions The SDSS, 2MASS and Spitzer data have a higher resolution than that of our model, which would correspond to 50 pc at the distance of M51. We therefore degrade the resolution of these data to match the resolution of our model using a two dimensional Gaussian kernel.

We discuss the properties of the faint and non axi-symmetric emission extending beyond R_ l , also containing the bridge between M51 and its companion M51b. We model this outer emission with another morphological component that we call outer disc . The outer disc has SFR =0.57 0.06 and _ SFR =5.11 0.56 10^ 4 .

sources.summary: The paper models M51B in the Whirlpool interaction, describing its tidal disturbances, deblending from M51A, three-component fit, asymmetric tidal feature, and bulge-to-disk results.

quotes.quote: The classical Whirlpool galaxy is a beautiful system where a grand-design spiral, M51A, is interacting with another spiral, M51B (Figure m51 , Table m51-table ). In addition to there being obvious spiral structures for both galaxies, there are large tidal disturbances that emerge from M51B, as seen in the SDSS band image provided by D. Finkbeiner. Because they are closely overlapping, a desirable goal is to deblend M51A and B, as well as to model the spiral and tidal structures, simultaneously.

For M51B, we employ three components in the fit, a bulge (component 5 in Table m51-table , top), a tidal feature component (component 6), and a spiral function (component 7), which model the three most visually striking components. The tidal feature is mostly obtained by using the second and third bending modes of Equation eqn:bending , as illustrated in Figure fig:bending . However, bending modes 2 and 3 are symmetric functions, so the high degree of asymmetry comes about because of combined action with the Fourier modes, which is shown to have a high amplitude of 0.23 for the m=1 mode, as well as moderate values for other modes.
In the most detailed analysis, the B/D ratio for M51A is 0.16, whereas for M51B it is merely 0.17.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5195 as a bright background nucleus probing M51 gas, measuring absorption, DIBs, stellar and emission velocities, and physical conditions along the line of sight.

quotes.quote: We present moderately-high resolution echelle observations of the nucleus of NGC 5195, the line of sight to which samples intervening interstellar material associated with the outer spiral arm of M51. Our spectra reveal absorption from interstellar Na i , K i , Ca ii , and CH ^+ , and from a number of diffuse interstellar bands (DIBs), at a velocity close to that exhibited by H i 21 cm emission from M51 at the position of NGC 5195. The H i column density implied by the equivalent width of the 5780.5 DIB, based on the relationship between W_ (5780.5) and N (H i ) derived for sight lines in the local Galactic interstellar medium, is consistent with the column density obtained from the integrated H i emission. The H _2 column density predicted from the observed column density of K i , using the Galactic relationship between N (K i ) and N (H _2 ), is comparable to N (H i ), suggesting a high molecular fraction ( 0.65) for the M51 gas toward NGC 5195. The DIBs toward NGC 5195 are, on average, 40 weaker than would be expected based on the K i column density, a further indication that the gas in this direction has a high molecular content.

For NGC 5195, the mean centroid velocity of the stellar absorption lines listed in Table 1 is 616 9 km s ^ 1 , while the mean velocity of the emission lines is 570 7 km s ^ 1 . (Errors for mean values reported in this paper are 1 standard deviations.) The overall mean for the emission lines and stellar absorption lines together is 588 25 km s ^ 1 , which is in good agreement with the velocity listed for NGC 5195 in the Updated Zwicky Catalog (UZC): v_ LSR =584 43 km s ^ 1 (Falco et al. 1999, applying a heliocentric-to-LSR correction of 12 km s ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5195 across radio, optical, and X-ray bands, finding extended synchrotron emission, shock-ionized arcs and bubbles, and evidence for past AGN jet activity beyond current star formation power.

quotes.quote: We studied the nearby, interacting galaxy NGC 5195 (M 51b) in the radio, optical and X-ray bands. We mapped the extended, low-surface-brightness features of its radio-continuum emission; determined the energy content of its complex structure of shock-ionized gas; constrained the current activity level of its supermassive nuclear black hole. In particular, we combined data from the European Very Long Baseline Interferometry Network ( 1-pc scale), from our new e-MERLIN observations ( 10-pc scale), and from the Very Large Array ( 100--1000-pc scale), to obtain a global picture of energy injection in this galaxy.

We find steep-spectrum, extended emission within 10 pc of the nuclear position, consistent with optically-thin synchrotron emission from nuclear star formation or from an outflow powered by an active galactic nucleus (AGN). A linear spur of radio emission juts out of the nuclear source towards the kpc-scale arcs (detected in radio, H and X-ray bands). From the size, shock velocity, and Balmer line luminosity of the kpc-scale bubble, we estimate that it was inflated by a long-term-average mechanical power 3--6 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 over the last 3--6 Myr. This is an order of magnitude more power than can be provided by the current level of star formation, and by the current accretion power of the supermassive black hole.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5195 as M 51's lenticular companion and LINER, quantifying how its radiation heats dust in nearby NGC 5194 while contributing little at larger radii.

quotes.quote: One of the most famous mergers in the local Universe is that of the grand-design spiral arm galaxy NGC 5194 (M 51a or the Whirlpool galaxy), and its companion, lenticular galaxy NGC 5195 (M 51b). The morphological type of M 51a is Sbc, and that of M 51b is SB0 de_Vaucouleurs_1995yCat.7155....0D . The centre of M 51a harbours a weak active galactic nucleus (AGN) classified as a Seyfert 2 Terashima_2001ApJ...560..139T, Bradley_2004ApJ...603..463B , while M 51b is classified as a LINER Satyapal_2004A&A...414..825S, Moustakas_2010ApJS..190..233M .

Furthermore, we quantify the contribution of the various dust heating sources in the system, and find that the young stellar population of NGC 5194 is the predominant dust-heating agent, with a global heating fraction of 71.2 . Another 23 is provided by the older stellar population of the same galaxy, while the remaining 5.8 has its origin in NGC 5195. Locally, we find that the regions of NGC 5194 closer to NGC 5195 are significantly affected by the radiation field of the latter, with the absorbed energy fraction rising up to 38 . The contribution of NGC 5195 remains under the percentage level in the outskirts of the disc of NGC 5194.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5195 as M51's interacting companion, quantifies its CO luminosity and molecular mass, and excludes noisy HCN map-edge regions containing it from later analyses.

quotes.quote: The tidal interaction of M51a with its companion, M51b (NGC 5195), is likely responsible for exciting the strong spiral density waves Dobbs:2010 .

This total CO luminosity includes contributions from NGC 5195. The total molecular mass is (9.32 0.01 ) 10 ^9 \ using the to-CO conversion factor _ CO =4.3 /(Kkms ^ 1 pc ^2 ) Bolatto:2013 . The CO luminosity of just NGC 5195 is (9.93 0.01) 10 ^7 Kkms ^ 1 pc ^2 , which corresponds to a molecular mass of (4.27 0.04 ) 10 ^8 . The CARMA-NRO CO imaging estimated the total mass of NGC 5194 and NGC 5195 to be 6.0 10 ^9 \ applying the same CO- \ conversion factor and distance as used in this study Koda:2011 .

For areas with declinations south of 47 ^ 07 \ and north of 47 ^ 15 \ that includes NGC 5195, the HCN noise levels are large due to less data collected near these map edges. These areas are not included in any subsequent analyses presented here.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5195 as M51's companion, whose encounter and gravitational perturbation generated tidal arms, bridge structures, eastern density-wave asymmetries, and other parts of M51's observed morphology.

quotes.quote: Among spiral galaxies, one of the most extensively observed object is undoubtedly the system of M51 (NGC 5194) and its companion NGC 5195. The first galaxy in which spiral structure was observed since 1845, this system has been studied in every e.m. wavelengths with ground based telescopes, balloon borne and space instrumentation. Several studies in morphology, spectroscopy, photometry, and polarimetry has been made in order to define the dynamic, the chemical constitution in stars, dust and gas, and the origin and evolution of the spectacular spiral pattern. The system was first suspected to be gravitationally interacting by Zwicky (1953), but the confirm was due to the pioneering works of Toomre Toomre (1972). The numerical simulations of these authors, starting from an undifferentiated disk, reproduced the formation of outer arms, tails and bridge structures, demonstrating a gravitational interaction with the companion.

The hypothesis of Elmgreen is that the inner density waves were triggered from the tidal waves due to the encounter with the companion. Howard Byrd on the contrary, with the results of a numerical self-gravitating simulation suggested that the large outer HI regions detected in the radio from Rots et al. (1990) can be originated by a precedent encounter of M51 with the same companion.

sources.summary: Chandra data show NGC5195 has extended X-ray emission offset from the optical center; the weak nuclear source contributes little compared with diffuse and off-nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: NGC5195 In the case of NGC5195 we observe extended X-ray emission peaking 5 arcsec away from the optical (UV) center of the galaxy. In particular, the X-ray emission peaks at =13h29m59.5s , =+47d15m57s while the UV nuclear coordinates are =13h29m59.2s , =+47d15m59s (Maoz et al. 1996). The coordinates of the nucleus in the radio are =13h29m59.5s , =+47d15m57s according to the NASA Extragalactic Database, (based on VLA observations by Ho Ulvestad 2001), coincident with the X-ray peak (offset 0.1 arcsec). Given the limited photon statistics it is impossible to discriminate whether this peak corresponds to an additional point source. The nuclear X-ray source has 10 counts (0.5-7 keV) in 2 arcsec region (assuming it is pointlike), translating to a luminosity of L_ 2-10 keV 2 10^ 38 .

Thus it contributes only a small fraction of the total galaxy X-ray emission. The brightest source has a luminosity of L_ 2-10 keV 3 10^ 38 while strong diffuse X-ray emission can be clearly seen up to 40 arcsec (1 kpc) with L_ 2-10 keV 10^ 39 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5195 as M51's interacting companion, classifies it as an SB0/I0 peculiar galaxy with a nonthermal nucleus, and cites models of disk passages through M51.

quotes.quote: The galaxy is known to be undergoing an interaction with its companion NGC 5195, and shows an active system of star formation (SF), largely confined to two spiral arms. The morphology of NGC 5194 is listed by de Vaucouleurs 1976 as a SA(s)bc(pec)III galaxy. The companion has been determined to be an SB0 (Sandage Tammann 1987), and as an I0(pec) by de Vaucouleurs, and its nucleus hosts a nonthermal source (Ford et al. 1985).

This interaction has been modelled extensively throughout the years, with increasing success in matching the present state of the M51/NGC 5195 system. Early models fit optical data well, but were unable to explain M51's extended HI tail (Toomre Toomre 1972; Hernquist 1990; Rots et al. 1990). More recent attempts fit HI maps well, but differ in the histories they paint for the M51 system. Salo Laurikainen's (2000) N-body model suggests elliptical orbits and multiple passages of NGC 5195 through the disk of M51.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC5195 as M51's nearby companion, whose interaction with NGC5194 and location near spectral-index features shape the interpretation of M51's outer disk.

quotes.quote: In the case of M51, many numerical simulations have been performed to study the consequences of the gravitational interaction between the main galaxy, NGC5194, and its nearby companion NGC5195 2000MNRAS.319..377S,2003Ap&SS.284..495T .
In the range 4 kpc < 10 kpc the spectral index gently decreases with a slight upturn at around 10 kpc, signifying the location of the companion galaxy NGC5195.

The outer disk of M51 towards the companion galaxy NGC5195 behaves differently. The region between the companion and the northern arm of M51 has a spectral index of 0.8 . The northern spiral arm continues far beyond the optical arm to the left of the companion. The arc shaped structure located just below NGC5195 seen in radio continuum 2011AJ....141...41D-1 and in H ii 1998ApJ...506..135G has a spectral index of 0.74 .

sources.summary: M51b is described as the dwarf companion in the interacting M51 pair, with LINER optical classification, AGN-powered mid-infrared emission, and a faint hard-X-ray nucleus.

quotes.quote: M51, first cataloged by , consists of a pair of interacting galaxies: M51a (NGC 5194), a grand-design spiral galaxy, first to be classified as a spiral galaxy, and M51b (NGC 5195), a dwarf galaxy. Indeed M51a and M51b are classed as a dual AGN: M51a hosts a Seyfert 2 in its nucleus stauffer82 that is obscured by Compton-thick (CT) material along the line of sight >1.5 10^ 24 fukazawa01 . Although M51b is classified as a LINER in the optical devaucouleurs91 , the Spitzer /IRS detection of [ Ne v ] 14.32 m confirms that M51b is AGN powered goulding09 .

The AGN in M51b has been estimated to have an X-ray luminosity of 10^ 39 \ in the 2--10 keV band hgarcia14 . Here we use ACIS-I with the aim point between the galaxies in order to better resolve the nucleus of M51b. For the nucleus of M51b, the differing off-axis angles and PSF sizes that have resulted from these in the previous observations make assessing the variability challenging so we do not co-add the \ data of M51b.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5195 as M 51's companion, showing diffuse emission, central point sources, a bright off-nuclear source, and one isolated ULX suitable for spectral study.

quotes.quote: Also present is the companion galaxy NGC 5195 showing diffuse emission, multiple sources in the central region, and a bright point source to the left of the nucleus.
One of the spiral arm of NGC 5194 appears to be tidally connected to the companion galaxy NGC 5195.

There is also a well isolated ULX in the companion galaxy NGC 5195. However, the central region of NGC 5195 consists of multiple bright point sources and extended soft emission as seen in the ACIS-S image (Terashima Wilson 2004). Due to lower angular resolution of , it is not possible to extract counts for individual sources in the central region of NGC 5195. Therefore we exclude the bright sources in the central region of NGC 5195 from our analysis.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5195 as M51's fainter SB0 companion, emphasizing faint fuzzy clusters concentrated around it and an elongated distribution interpreted as tidal debris with NGC 5194.

quotes.quote: M51 is an interacting galaxy system that is composed of a face-on Sbc-type spiral galaxy NGC 5194 and a fainter companion, the SB0 galaxy NGC 5195.

lee05 later discovered that there are faint fuzzy clusters around NGC 5195, a companion galaxy of NGC 5194. Based on HST ACS data, hwa06 identified 49 faint fuzzy clusters around NGC 5195 and also reported that these large clusters display a different spatial distribution pattern, compared to the compact clusters with similar colors, implying a different origin for these faint fuzzy clusters.

In hwa06 , the faint fuzzies in NGC 5195 are shown to display a very similar spatial distribution that is elongated along the same northeast-southwest direction. It is pointed out by hwa06 that the elongated distribution of the faint fuzzies may be a part of the debris of tidal interactions between NGC 5194 and NGC 5195.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 5195 as a mid-infrared AGN missed optically, with possible binary AGN activity and little host-galaxy extinction.

quotes.quote: Of the 18 galaxies in common between D09 and our sample, D09 identified [ NeV ] 14.32 in three systems (NGC 3621; NGC 5033; NGC 5194), all of which we also identify here; however, we also identified [ NeV ] emission in two systems where D09 quote [ NeV ] upper limits (NGC 3627; NGC 5195), which could be due to dilution of the [ NeV ] emission by the host galaxy in the large apertures used by D09. Ten of our mid-IR classified AGNs are not unambiguously identified as AGNs at optical wavelengths: five are classified as HII galaxies (NGC 0613, NGC 1448, NGC 1792, NGC 4945, and NGC 5128), four are classified as LINERS (NGC 0660, NGC 3627, NGC 3628, and NGC 5195), and one does not have good-quality optical spectroscopic data (ESO121-G006). One of the optically unidentified AGNs is NGC 5195, which may be a binary AGN system with NGC 5194 in the Whirlpool galaxy.

Potential AGN activity has also been found in NGC 5195 using \ observations terashima04 . Furthermore, three of the optically unidentified AGNs are found to be hosted in relatively face-on galaxies (NGC 0613, NGC 3627 and NGC 5195), similar to that of the optically identified AGNs, suggesting there is little extinction from the host galaxy towards these AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper treats M51b/NGC5195 as M51a's companion, linking 20 cm emission, an arc-shaped feature south of the companion, and faint northern emission to its ongoing interaction with M51a.

quotes.quote: Distinct 20 cm radio continuum emission is present between the northern arm of M51a and its companion. This region is less bright in the 6 cm map and is hardly evident in the 3.6 cm map. It is consistent with synchrotron emission that is enhanced by the ongoing interaction of NGC5195 with M51a (see Schinnerer et al. in prep. for a detailed analysis of this region). In particular an interesting feature in this region is the arc shaped structure south of M51b, also observed in H emission and Fig. fig:radio greenawalt_98 . North of NGC5195, extended faint emission is present at 20 cm and 6 cm.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5195 has variable XMM-Newton normalization and soft/hard flux changes, but no Chandra simultaneous-fit changes over three days.

quotes.quote: NGC 5195 : SMF1 is used for XMM -Newton data, where the best representation let Norm_2 vary (20 ). A flux variation of 9 (19 ) in the soft (hard) energy band is found in a period of eight years. In the case of Chandra data, no variations are found from the simultaneous fit (i.e., SMF0) in a three days period. The annular region contributes with 74 in Chandra data, so the data from the two instruments are not compared. The analysis of one Chandra light curve reveals short term variations in the soft and total bands below 2 confidence level.

sources.summary: The paper describes M51b as M51a's companion, with declining recent star formation, an old stellar population, and IR emission mainly powered by stars older than 5 Gyr.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Region Characteristics & M51a & M51b & outskirts & all _ J2000 & 202.47065 & 202.49726 & & _ J2000 & 47.19517 & 47.26531

quotes.quote: For M51b, we find a declining SFR toward the present day. In the outskirt regions of M51a, which includes regions between M51a and M51b, we recover a SFR peak between 0.1 and 1 Gyr ago, which corresponds to the effects of the interaction between M51a and M51b. We utilize our results to (1) illustrate how UV+IR hybrid SFR laws vary across M51, and (2) provide first-order estimates for how the IR luminosity per unit stellar mass varies as a function of the stellar age. From the latter result, we find that IR emission from dust heated by stars is not always associated with young stars, and that the IR emission from M51b is primarily powered by stars older than 5 Gyr.
M51b has the reddest stellar population and, at least apparently, the hottest dust emission in the FIR (from the highest ratio of 70 and 160 \ luminosities).
M51b has a predominantly old stellar population, with most of its mass coming from the oldest SFH step and considerably little SF activity over the last 100 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper treats M51b as M51's companion, linking it to M51's grand-design spiral structure, full observed field, stronger Arm 2 offsets, and possible spiral shocks from interaction.

quotes.quote: The grand--design spiral structure in M51 is thought to be caused by the tidal interaction between its companion galaxy M51b Toomre1972,Pettitt2017,Tress2019 .
We covered an area of 6 12 , extending over the full extent of M51 and its companion M51b.

The offsets between ^ 12 CO and [ C ii ] in M51 are more apparent in Arm 2, connecting to the companion galaxy M51b, compared with Arm 1, pointing away from the companion.
