The nitrogen abundance that we found is quite low for a galaxy of normal metallicity, as M 33 is, if the abundances of the (best observed) elements like He and O are compared with the corresponding values for the Galactic PNe.

sources.summary: The paper treats M33 as a nearby spiral target for an Arecibo giant-pulse search, emphasizing its distance, blue-supergiant population, full-galaxy beam coverage, and low expected dispersion.

quotes.quote: The spiral galaxy M33, at a distance of approximately 840 kpc , is an excellent candidate for giant-pulse detection because its light derives mainly from blue, supergiant stars (i.e. progenitors of neutron stars). It is also visible with Arecibo, currently the most sensitive instrument for single-pulse searches at meter wavelengths. Our Arecibo search used a radio frequency of 430 MHz, optimal because of the steep spectral indices of pulsars and the low dispersion and scattering expected towards M33. The entire galaxy was covered with 16 9' diameter beams, as shown in Figure fig:m33beams .

sources.summary: The paper describes M33 as a nearby inclined SA(s)cd spiral whose central ultraluminous source M33 X-8 shows properties inconsistent with an AGN and consistent with an unusual X-ray binary.

quotes.quote: M33 (NGC 598) is one of the nearest spiral galaxies ( d=795 kpc). Classified as SA(s)cd, it has an inclination angle of 55^ (Ho et al. 1997). Since the first observations with the Einstein satellite (Long et al. 1981), it was clear that the central source (M33 X-8) had particular features (luminosity in the 0.2-4 keV energy range of about 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 , soft spectrum, excess of absorption along the line of sight) suggesting that the source is somewhat different from an active galactic nucleus (Trinchieri et al. 1988). The authors suggested the possibility that M33 contains a new type of X-ray binary system.

sources.summary: The SKA paper uses M33 as an extragalactic astrometry example, citing VLBA maser proper motions, a rotation-model comparison, and a distance uncertainty of about 15 percent.

quotes.quote: Fig. 4 illustrate the results from recent VLBA observations which have measured the proper motion between two H _2 O maser sources that lie on the opposite sides of M33. The derived distance estimate is already better than 15 .

Fig 4: The Motion of Two Maser Sources in M33: The arrows show the expected motion of the maser source IC133 (100 km/s) and M33/19 (62 km/s) with respect to the radio core, derived from the known rotation model for the galaxy. The radio observations of the relative motion of the two maser sources were made over a 2.5 year period and give a distance uncertainty to M33 of 15 .

sources.summary: The paper links M33 to a diffuse HI filament connected with M31 and states that the structure ends near both galaxies in apparent ongoing accretion features.

quotes.quote: On even larger scales, there is evidence for large-scale accretion of gas along ``cosmic web'' filaments providing continued fueling of both M31 and M33.

In addition to detecting many of the discrete features noted above within about 50 kpc of M31, a diffuse filament is detected connecting the systemic velocities of M31 and M33 and extending beyond M31 in the anti-M33 direction, as seen in Fig. m31m33 . The total extent of this feature is some 260 kpc. In the immediate vicinity of both M31 and M33 it terminates in what appear to be ongoing accretion features.

sources.summary: The review reports GALEX radial-profile work on M33, finding UV emission more extended than ionized gas and a mostly constant FUV diffuse fraction across much of the galaxy.

quotes.quote: M31, M32 and M33: Radial Gradients For M31 and M33 (Figures 3 4, respectively) Thilker et al. have determined the UV radial light and color profiles and compared them with the distribution of ionized gas as traced by H emission. It is found that the extent of the UV emission, in both targets, is greater than the extent of the observed HII regions and diffuse ionized gas. In addition, the ultraviolet diffuse fraction in M33 using the FUV observations has been compared to the H diffuse fraction obtained from wide-field narrow-band imaging.

The FUV diffuse fraction appears to be remarkably constant near 0.65 over a large range in galactocentric radius, with departures to higher values in circumnuclear regions and, most notably, at the limit of the H disk.

sources.summary: The paper presents an M33 variability survey that goes deeper and covers essentially the entire galaxy, detecting 36,709 variable sources and greatly increasing its known variable-star census.

quotes.quote: Our present survey has two improvements for finding variables over previous surveys of M33. The first is that by using a 3.6 metre telescope we go deeper than any previous survey. The second is that we are able to cover a much wider field (essentially the entire galaxy) than previously possible for CCD surveys. As a result we have detected 36709 variable sources which represents an increase by more than a factor of 20 in the number of known variables in the field of M33.

sources.summary: The paper describes M33's faint extended disk, detected halo, and dramatic outer-disk truncation, contrasting its lack of tidal streams with M31's more complex outer disk.

quotes.quote: The disks in M31 and M33 have been detected at _V = 30 mag arcsec ^ 2 and are seen to extend to more then 10 scale lengths. The metal-poor stellar halo in M31 is now thought to extend to at least 160 kpc (Guhathakurta et al 2005), i.e. a halo subtending 20 ^ across the skyThe halo of M33 has now been detected and found to be comparable in metallicity to M31 and the Galaxy (McConnachie et al 2006), which is interesting in light of the fact that it has no detectable bulge.

This galaxy is a virtual twin of M33 but showing a different behaviour in the outer parts. The red stellar disk is exponential over 10 scale lengths with no evidence for a truncation of any kind (Bland-Hawthorn et al 2005). In contrast, M33 shows dramatic truncation close to the Holmberg radius with no evidence of tidal streams (see below), quite unlike its dominant neighbour.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes M33 AGB stars in elliptical sectors, finding a 7-8 Gyr metal-poor stellar population and high C/M ratios tracing major spiral arms.

quotes.quote: For example, the subdivision of the area covered by M33 comprises 3500 O-rich AGB stars and 300 C-rich AGB stars in each of five concentric ellipses; a histogram was created for each of eight sectors per ellipse.

The stellar population of M33 derived from observations obtained at the UKIRT telescope appears on average 7-8 Gyr old and it is metal poor. The distribution of high C/M ratio values, corresponding to a lower metallicity ( ), traces the structure of the major spiral arms presenting a puzzle to its interpretation.

The effect of differential reddening and of the structure of the galaxy on the C/M ratio is negligible while it is fundamental in the distribution of metallicity and SFR obtained from the number density of AGB stars.

sources.summary: The paper argues that M33 is tightly bound to M31 and may have undergone tidal deformation, motivating searches for observational evidence of that interaction.

quotes.quote: The M31 transverse velocity implies that M33 is in a tightly bound orbit around M31. This may have led to some tidal deformation of M33. It will be worthwhile to search for observational evidence of this.

sources.summary: The paper measures M33 disk and outer halo metallicities and interprets the gradient as evidence for inside-out disk formation through accretion of metal-poor gas.

quotes.quote: The gradient of the M33 disc, until 9 kpc, is 0.078 0.003 dex kpc ^ 1 while an outer disc/halo, out to 25 kpc, has [ Fe/H ] 1.7 dex.
The metallicity of M33 supports an ``inside-out'' disc formation via accretion of metal poor gas from the interstellar medium.
This provides evidence for an inside-out formation scenario for the M33 disc also supported by simulations.

sources.summary: The paper treats M33 as a prototype late-type disk with ongoing star formation, modeled cluster populations, and central light excesses relevant to nuclear star cluster growth.

quotes.quote: To this end, we develop a crude toy model to calculate the influx of migrating young clusters formed in a galaxy that has experienced an instantaneous star formation episode, but our results are relevant for galaxies, like the prototypical late-type disk galaxy M33, in which star formation has been steady and ongoing over the life of the galaxy, although the timescale for the accumulation of NSC mass will differ from the one estimated here because of the dependence, in this case, on the star formation rate in the disk.
Lamers:05a modeled the observed cluster population in M33 and found, at an arbitrary radius, t_0 f_ dis / (R) with f_ dis,M33 0.16 .

Nevertheless, the excess we observe in the central one or few hundred parsecs is suggestive of pseudobulges that have been identified photometrically in disk galaxies Kormendy:04,Fisher:08,Fisher:09,Fisher:10,Weinzirl:09 and the ``central light excesses'' identified in bulgeless disks Boker:03 , including the late-type-disk M33 Kent:87,Minniti:93 .

sources.summary: The paper describes M33 as surrounded by a large low-surface-brightness structure extending to about 40 kpc and favors tidal disruption in its orbit around M31.

quotes.quote: We have previously shown that the disk of M33 is surrounded by a large, irregular, low-surface brightness substructure.
At these low surface brightness levels, the structure extends to projected radii of 40 kpc from the center of M33 in both the north-west and south-east quadrants of the galaxy.

Our favored model is that of the tidal disruption of M33 in its orbit around M31. galaxies: halos --- galaxies: individual (M33, M31) --- galaxies: interactions --- Local Group --- galaxies: structure noted that they had succeeded in ``turning three puzzles into one enigma'' with their analysis of the Local Group spiral galaxy M33 (the Triangulum Galaxy).

sources.summary: The review uses the M31-M33 stellar bridge as nearby evidence that low-surface-brightness tidal debris can reveal underestimated galaxy interactions.

quotes.quote: See however on Figure fig:M31 the stellar bridge between Andromeda and M33. The debris, of extremely low surface brightness, are remnants of a tidal interaction between two of the most massive galaxies in our Local Group. Therefore the evaluation of the merger rate based on statistics on galaxy pairs should consider systems like M31/M33 as pairs even-though the two galaxies have a spatial separation has high as 220 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper discusses M33 as an observational outlier with a large magnetic-field pitch angle, smaller than its optical-arm pitch angle and difficult for the models to explain.

quotes.quote: However, there is at least one notable exception: the pitch angle in M33 is inferred from observations to be 40^ Tabatabaei+08 .
Typical pitch angles of the regular magnetic field in M33, though large in magnitude compared to other galaxies, are significantly smaller in magnitude than those of the optical arms Fletcher10 .
However, extreme cases with |p_B| as large as 40^ as in the galaxy M33 and near-perfect alignment of magnetic and optical arms as in NGC 6946 are clearly not possible to explain using such models.

sources.summary: The paper models M33 supernova-remnant size distributions, finding a warm ISM filling factor near 90 percent and estimating the galaxy's SNR birthrate and population.

quotes.quote: We found that a recently published collection of SNRs in M33 satisfies this requirement if we select the X-ray SNRs with hardness ratios in a limited range of values. An excellent agreement between distributions of this subset of SNRs and the subset of modeled SNRs was reached for a volume filling-factor of the warm phase of the ISM (partly ionized gas with n_ H 0.2-0.5 cm ^ 3 ; T 8000-10000 K ) in M33 of \ 90 . The statistical distributions constructed in this way, which reproduce practically all the statistical properties of observed SNRs, allowed us to obtain one of the important parameters of M33: the birthrate is one SNR every 140 150 yr, and the total number of SNRs with a shock Mach number M_ s 2 is larger than 1000 .

sources.summary: The paper compares M 33 with IC 342, noting ill-defined material arms, a weak northern magnetic arm, comparable radio-continuum resolution limits, and similar outer-disk magnetic-energy behavior.

quotes.quote: M 33 has ill-defined material arms and only a radio-weak magnetic arm in the northern region taba08 .

Similar spatial resolutions in radio continuum have been achieved at 20 in M 31 beck98 and in M 33 taba07a , but magnetic field investigations were hampered by Faraday depolarization that is strong in that wavelength band.

Dynamo action in the outer disk In IC 342 (like in NGC 6946 and M 33) the magnetic energy density decreases more slowly than the turbulent energy density and dominates beyond about 5 \ radius, while models of field amplification by small-scale and mean-field dynamos predict a close similarity between these energy components beck96,gent13 .

sources.summary: The review cites M 33 for ISM magnetic-field orientation, thermal/nonthermal radio separation, and ordered fields in a flocculent spiral.

quotes.quote: It was also observed in the ISM of the galaxy M 33 li11 , where it is consistent with the field orientations along the spiral arms as measured by polarized radio continuum emission taba08 . The 90^ ambiguity needs to be solved with help of additional continuum polarization data.
The advanced method to separate the two components introduced by taba07 uses an extinction-corrected H image as a template for the thermal radio emission and was first applied to the galaxy M 33.

Ordered magnetic fields with strengths similar to those in grand-design spiral galaxies have been detected in the flocculent galaxies M 33 taba08 , NGC 3521 and NGC 5055 knapik00 , and in NGC 4414 soida02 .

sources.summary: The paper incorporates prior Spitzer H II-region data for M33, describing it as a Local Group spiral with lower metallicity and higher ionization than M83.

quotes.quote: Previously we had taken measurements of emission lines in 24 H ii regions in the substantially face-on spiral galaxy M83 (Rubin et al. 2007, hereafter R07) that we observed with Spitzer and of 25 H ii regions in the Local Group spiral galaxy M33 (Rubin et al. 2008, hereafter R08), also observed with Spitzer . Here we combine our measurements and derived quantities from those studies with Spitzer data from 9 H ii regions in the Local Group dwarf irregular galaxy NGC 6822 in order to more fully examine trends that resulted from our previous analysis.
We concluded (R08) that the lower Ne/S values derived for the M33 H ii regions are more reliable estimates because M33 has lower metallicity and higher ionization than M83.

sources.summary: The paper uses M 33 dust maps as a vigorous star-forming local-galaxy template to evaluate how the CMB alters cold-dust continuum measurements.

quotes.quote: We exploit NGC 628, M 33, and M 31, for which there are high-quality T_ dust maps over most of their extent and whose levels of star-formation activity range from the vigorous, in NGC 628 and M 33 ( _ SFR 10^ 3 10^ 2 \ - ), to the quiescent M 31 ( _ SFR 10^ 4 10^ 3 \ - ).
The M 33 data were observed in the HerM33es project Herschel M33 extended survey: http://www.iram.es/IRAMES/hermesWiki/FrontPage (P.I. Kramer; observation ID 1342189079).

We simplify the fitting procedures in the literature and repeat the fit to derive cold dust temperature maps for M 31, M 33, and NGC 628, by fixing the emissivity indices for both cold and warm components.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts M33 with NGC 300 and NGC 2403, describing it as less isolated, warped by interaction with M31, connected by gas streams, and having a broken stellar profile.

quotes.quote: M33 is the least isolated, containing a warped HI disk likely due to a weak interaction with M31.

For example, gas streams lying along the axis between M33 and M31 are indicative of a past interaction 2009ApJ...703.1486P,2013ApJ...763....4L , whereas NGC 300 and NGC 55 have no strong commonalities.

M33 is known to have a break in the stellar surface brightness profile while those of NGC 2403 and NGC 300 are unbroken as far as can be measured 2005ApJ...629..239B, 2007iuse.book..239F, 2012MNRAS.419.1489B .

sources.summary: The paper adopts M33 as the source galaxy for nanolensing searches, emphasizing its distance, face-on spiral structure, diffuse arms, and suitability for weak-amplification monitoring.

quotes.quote: As a source galaxy, we adopt a spiral galaxy M33 at 840 kpc freedman91 .

Although M33 is more compact and less massive than M31, the flux contribution from faint unresolved stars per pixel is expected to be smaller than M31 as M33 is a typical face-on galaxy with much diffuse spiral arm where bright A- and F-type main sequence stars are relatively abundant.
Therefore, for the purpose of observing weak amplification of bright resolved stars, M33 would be much suitable.

sources.summary: The paper includes M 33 among the massive actors because it has measured proper motion; its model mass is close to catalog, but zeroing it disrupts some tracer fits.

quotes.quote: IC 10 and M 33 are even less luminous, but since they are particularly interesting because they have measured proper motions they are included among the twelve massive actors. Since their model masses are close to catalog values I suspect they could have been treated as massless with little effect on the model. (This is difficult to check without repeating the whole computation, because setting the masses of IC 10 and M 33 to zero in the present solution seriously disturbs the redshifts and distances of some of the tracers, though it only modestly affects the other massive actors.)

sources.summary: The paper cites M33 as a previous high-sensitivity H I case with a reported second radial profile break at low column density along a major-axis slice.

quotes.quote: Using single-dish observations with the Arecibo telescope, corbellietal89 examined the outer radial H i distribution of the northern part of the major axis of M33 down to a (3 detection limit) column density of 2 10 ^ 18 cm ^ 2 . The column density is 2 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 in the central disk and the value drops below 2 10 ^ 20 cm ^ 2 just outside the optical disk, which then gradually declined to 3 10 ^ 19 cm ^ 2 . A second break in the radial profile was observed at 3 10 ^ 19 cm ^ 2 , below which the value decreased to 2 10 ^ 18 cm ^ 2 within 1 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper uses M33 galaxy parameters to estimate mirror magnetic-field evolution, plots the modeled field with M31, and argues mirror fields matter inside about 5 kpc.

quotes.quote: As examples, for M33 galaxy by substituting v'=120 km s ^ 1 , M'=6 10^ 10 M_ , R_ g0 =17 kpc , we obtain a_0/a_*=10.1 , _*'=3.5 10^ 16 s ^ 1 , t_*=1.7 10^ 16 s . For M31 galaxy v'=190 km s ^ 1 , M'=1.2 10^ 12 M_ , R_ g0 =35 kpc and we get the following values: a_0/a_*=7.0, _*'=7.5 10^ 16 s ^ 1 , t_*=3.0 10^ 16 s . The dependence of the mirror magnetic field on parameter x for M33 and M31 galaxies at the moment of the inhomogeneity separation is shown in figure graph2 .
It is clear that the mirror magnetic field becomes significant at small radii where u' v'_ orb . According to rotation curves for M31 and M33 this happens at radii less than 5 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper uses M33 low central compact-object limit and halo rotation properties as the strongest bulgeless-galaxy constraint on ultra-light axion core models.

quotes.quote: Finally, M33 (a nearby spiral galaxy embedded in a dark matter halo) has an asymptotic circular velocity of V_ circ 125 mayall/aller:1942 and the tightest upper limit on the SMBH mass: M_ < 1.5 10^3 gebhardt/etal:2001 .
The absence of a compact object at the center of M33 gives the strongest constraint: m>1.2 10^ 18 (or, equivalently, m_ 22 >1.2 10^4 ).

In particular, the non-detection of a central compact object in M33 -- an isolated spiral galaxy from the local group without any indication of recent mergers or interactions with other galaxies verley/etal:2009 - gives m 1.2 10^ 18 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M33 as a nearby example of a bulge-free galaxy with a nuclear stellar cluster but no evidence for a supermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: M33 is a local example. This galaxy is bulge-free, which suggests a relatively quiet history without any major mergers. We therefore conclude that M33 likely has never possessed a supermassive black hole.

sources.summary: M33 is framed as a nearby flocculent spiral laboratory for ALMA studies of gas dynamics, star formation, and giant molecular filaments in its spiral arm.

quotes.quote: Although the achievable spatial resolution is more than 10 times coarser than that in the Magellanic clouds, the Triangulum Galaxy (M33) provides us with a unique laboratory located nearest to us, 840 kpc Freedman01 , among similar targets accessible from the ALMA site. The flocculent spiral galaxy, M33, has the potential to investigate the effects of the complex gas dynamics on star formation and galaxy evolution Wada11 , which may not be achieved by studies of `` grand design " spiral galaxies, such as M51. Our M33 observations with the ALMA found giant molecular filaments whose length is more than 50 pc along one of the stellar spiral arm ( Tokuda20 , hereafter Paper I).

sources.summary: The systemic proper motion of M33 is somewhat sensitive to the chosen stellar-selection region, but the change is comparable to systematic error.

quotes.quote: M33 The systemic PM of M 33 seems to be somewhat sensitive to the spatial region used for the selection of the stars to be analyzed. If we were to use a region within a semi-major axis radius of 0.2 , the _ ,* component would remain practically unchanged, whilst the _ component would decrease to 0.009 0.005 mas yr ^ 1 . However, it should be noticed that this change does not appear significant, since it is of the size of the systematic error related to a scale length of 0.6 , and this systematic error would increase when considering a smaller spatial region.

sources.summary: The paper assigns M33 an independent mass, notes B-band scaling would overestimate it, and includes it in calculations of nearby-galaxy dispersion and scattering contributions.

quotes.quote: For the four galaxies with the largest angular extents on the sky, the LMC, SMC, M31, and M33, we adopt independent mass measurements M_ LMC = 1.7 10^ 10 M_ , M_ SMC = 2.4 10^9M_ 2016ARAA..54..363D , M_ M31 = 1.5 10^ 12 M_ 2012ApJ...753....8V , M_ M33 = 10^ 11.72 M_ 2017AJ....154...41K , and we note that scaling the mass by the absolute B- band magnitude provided by GWGC overestimates the mass of M33 by a factor of two. The DM and scattering time contributions of each galaxy were calculated using the density model described in Section  sec:density_modeling , with each galaxy type assigned by the Hubble T-type listed in GWGC: T-types between 6 and 0 were modeled as elliptical galaxies, T-types between 0 and 9 as spiral galaxies, and T-types between 9 and 10 as dwarf galaxies. The inclination angle of each galaxy was calculated directly from GWGC, while the azimuthal angle for each LOS was drawn from a uniform distribution between 0^ and 90^ .

sources.summary: The paper links M33 to Tucana through orbital-plane alignment and a possible early pericentric passage around M31 that could have ejected Tucana.

quotes.quote: Further, Tucana is located close to M33's orbital plane around M31, and simple orbit integrations indicate that Tucana may have been ejected during an early pericentric passage of M33 11 Gyr ago, a timing which approximately coincides with Tucana's last episode of star formation. We suggest that Tucana may have been an early-infalling satellite of M31 or M33, providing a compelling explanation for its puzzling lack of gas and ongoing star formation despite its isolated nature. In this scenario, M33 should have completed some orbits around M31, a result that may help to explain the relative dearth of M33 satellite-candidates identified so far.

sources.summary: The paper says M33 is a well-studied Local Group distance-calibrator comparison, with Mira classifications and a Mira distance modulus of 24.80 mag.

quotes.quote: M33 M33 is another Local Group galaxy that has been extensively analyzed in variability studies and thus offers interesting comparisons between independent distance calibrators.

, which focused on the classification of the Miras in M33, the first to fit the Mira P-L using a quadratic relation and was the first to derive a distance to M33 using Miras with both linear and quadratic P-L relations.
From this they obtained _ M33 = 24.80 0.06 mag.

sources.summary: The paper uses 200 nm imaging of M33 to measure disk morphology, finding an exponential-like UV profile and that the adopted evolutionary scenario still leaves S/N below 10.

quotes.quote: We have used ultraviolet (200 nm) images of the local spiral galaxies M33, M51, M81, M100, M101 to compute morphological parameters of galactic disks at this wavelength : half-light radius r_ hl , surface brightness distributions, asymmetries ( A ) and concentrations ( C_A ).
Whereas M33 and M101 exhibit a rather linear profile, as expected for exponential disks, M51 and M81 have a non-monotonic distribution and M100 may be viewed as an intermediate case.
The adopted evolutionary scenario is not enough for M33 and S/N < 10.

sources.summary: The paper uses M33 as a counterexample in black-hole hierarchy, noting its low central black-hole limit, young stellar population, and possible inability to retain enough ISM for massive black-hole growth.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, M33 appears not to have a massive BH with an upper limit of 1500 M_ (Gebhardt et al. 2001). An intriguing difference is that M33 contains a significant population of stars younger than a few Gyrs (e.g., O'Connell 1983), in contrast to other globular clusters which possess massive BHs. Thus, M33 might not have been able to retain a sufficient ISM owing to successive supernova explosions.

sources.summary: The review presents M33 as a bulgeless disk whose central star cluster lacks the black hole expected from spheroid velocity-dispersion scaling, with possible explanations including nonlinear scaling or its young nucleus.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, the nondetection of a central black hole in the central star cluster of M33 contrasts with the G1 case and argues for the absence of massive black holes in the centers of the distinct nuclei of bulgeless disks. Gebhardt et al. \ (2001) discuss that, if the mass of a central black hole in the nucleus of M33 was related to its velocity dispersion in the same way that the known supermassive black holes are related to the dispersions of their bulges, then a black hole with mass in the range 7 10^3-6 10^4 M_ would be expected, well above the measured upper limit of 1500 M_ . Solutions to this inconsistency include those suggested by the authors: the relationship between the mass of the black hole and the velocity dispersion of the host spheroid may be nonlinear; the conditions to make a massive black hole were better in the earlier, denser Universe, when the stars in G1 were made; or M33's young nucleus has not had enough time to create its own black hole.

sources.summary: The paper includes M33 among Local Group annihilation targets, finding its predicted flux comparable to the Galactic foreground in some models but dimmer under an NFW profile.

quotes.quote: In some cases, like M33 and Sagittarius, extragalactic and Galactic contributions are comparable. Similar considerations are valid for the case of a NFW profile (filled dots), despite of the significant reduction of the extragalactic fluxes that cause M33 and Sagittarius to be dimmer than the Galactic foreground.
Indeed, among the LG galaxies only the Milky Way and M31 harbor a SMBH, while a possible black hole in M33 would have a mass smaller than 3000 .

sources.summary: The paper describes M33 as one endpoint of the Local Group H I filament; the filament connects to M31, extends relative to M33, and may feed gaseous streams in its outskirts.

quotes.quote: The first image of the Cosmic Web in HI emission has just been made of the Local Group filament connecting M31 and M33.

A diffuse filament is detected connecting the systemic velocities of M31 to M33 (at a projected seperation of 200 kpc) and also extending away from M31 in the anti-M33 direction as shown in Figure 1. This diffuse filament appears to be fueling denser gaseous streams and filaments in the outskirts of both galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 0598 as a rising-rotation-curve outlier whose velocity choice determines whether it fits the circular-velocity and bulge-dispersion relation.

quotes.quote: NGC 0598 was a clear outlier.
The v_c for NGC 0598 used in fer4 was 135 km s ^ 1 , which is the highest data point of a rising RC .
If the v_c = v_ rmax = 85 km s ^ 1 is used by fer4 for NGC 0598, NGC 0598 will fit on her plotted line.

sources.summary: The paper notes that M33 is a smaller Local Group spiral whose position is very close to the early-type-companion great circle but far from the plane fitted to all M31 satellites.

quotes.quote: The nearby spiral M33 has a distance of only 3 kpc from this plane, which points toward the M81 group.

Interestingly, also the smaller Local Group spiral M33 is directly encompassed by this great circle (its orthogonal distance to this plane being 2.8 kpc). However, it is not related to the great plane resulting from the fit to all M31 satellites -- here M33 has a distance of 135 kpc from the plane.

sources.summary: The paper describes M33 as the nearest Sc galaxy with a nuclear cluster, noting young central stars, variable star formation, and central-cluster mass estimates.

quotes.quote: M33 is the nearest Sc galaxy hosting a NC and its nucleus has been extensively studied in the past decade (recently e.g. in Davidge 2000; Long, Charles Dubus 2002; Stephens Frogel 2002). Despite some differences in the details, all studies of M33 agree that there is some population younger than 0.5 Gyr in the central parsec and that star formation has varied significantly over the past several Gyrs. The mass of the central cluster in M33 was estimated from a detailed population analysis in Gordon et al. (1999) to be 5 10^5 M _ , consistent with the upper limit derived from the velocity dispersion by Kormendy McClure (1993) of 2 10^6 M _ .

gmw_00000316.name: Abell 1836-BCG
gmw_00000316.overview: Abell 1836-BCG is a massive brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0610702, arXiv:2103.07478] classified as a type-1 LINER [arXiv:2012.07882] and an FR II-like radio galaxy [arXiv:1404.5703]. It hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:astro-ph/0610702] and exhibits broad nuclear emission lines associated with an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2012.07882].

sources.summary: The paper describes PGC 049940 as a BCG with broad nuclear emission, a redshifted high-dispersion component linked to AGN-driven flow, and type-1 LINER classification.

quotes.quote: PGC 049940 This galaxy shows very broad emission lines in the nuclear region, associated with the putative supermassive black hole ( 3.61 10^9 , DallaBonta2009 ).

The redshifted (of 300 with respected to the first one) component of PGC 049940 (Fig. fig:049940_2 ) is characterized by a velocity dispersion rising in the center up to nearly 500 \ and may be associated with an AGN-driven in/outflow of gas. It is more centrally concentrated than the first one even if the spatial scales are comparable. The broad ( 1000 ) component (Fig. fig:049940_3 ) results from the BLR of the host galaxy.

PGC 049940 can be classified as type-1 LINER HermosaMunoz2020 due to the presence of a broad H emission in the nucleus coming from the BLR. In this case we concluded that the AGN contribution to ionisation is strong in the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper observes Abell 1836-BCG with ACS and STIS, models its ionized-gas kinematics, and derives a dynamically measured supermassive black hole mass of 4.8e9 solar masses.

quotes.quote: We observed a sample of three Brightest Cluster Galaxies (BCGs), Abell 1836-BCG, Abell 2052-BCG, and Abell 3565-BCG, with the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) and the Imaging Spectrograph (STIS) on board the Space Telescope. For each target galaxy we obtained high-resolution spectroscopy of the H and [ N II ] 6583 emission lines at three slit positions, to measure the central ionized-gas kinematics. ACS images in three different filters (F435W, F625W, and FR656N) have been used to determine the optical depth of the dust, stellar mass distribution near the nucleus, and intensity map.
For the SBHs of Abell 1836-BCG and Abell 3565-BCG, we derived M_ =4.8^ +0.8 _ 0.7 10^9 M _ and M_ =1.3^ +0.3 _ 0.4 10^9 M _ at 1 confidence level, respectively.

For Abell 1836-BCG, we derive M_ =4.8^ +0.8 _ 0.7 10^9 M _ , the largest SBH mass to have been dynamically measured to-date. The best fit inclination angle is i=76^ 1^ , while only an upper limit to the stellar mass-to-light ratio is found ( M/L_I 4.0 M/L _I _ at 1 confidence level).

sources.summary: The paper maps PGC049940 with VEGAS and MUSE, finding the lowest accreted fraction in the trio, a second transition beyond the MUSE field, and a mildly increasing color profile.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present the new deep images from the VEGAS survey of three massive ( M_ * 10^ 12 M _ ) galaxies from the MUSE Most Massive Galaxies (M3G) project, with distances in the range 151 D 183 Mpc: PGC007748, PGC015524 and PGC049940. The long integration time and the wide field of view of OmegaCam@VST allowed us to map the light and color distributions down to _g 30 mag/arcsec ^2 and out to 2R_e .
Our results suggest that PGC049940 has the lowest accreted mass fraction of 77 .

For PGC049940 the second transition radius occurs at R > 60 arcsec, that is outside the galaxy regions covered by the MUSE field of view. For PGC007748 and PGC015524 instead, both the first ( R_ tr1 ) and the second ( R_ tr2 ) transition radii are within 60 arcsec. The azimuthally averaged (g - ) color profiles show a similar behaviour up to the first transition radius ( R_ tr2 ) for all the three galaxies (bottom panels of Fig. PGC7748 , PGC015524 and PGC049940 ). They all have redder colors in the central regions. A gradient towards bluer colors is observed from the galaxies' centers out to the first transition radius ( R_ tr1 ). For R_ tr1 R R_ tr2 the color profile of PGC007748 remains almost flat, while for PGC015524 it is slightly decreasing. The opposite happens for the color profile of PGC049940, which shows a mild increase in the region R_ tr1 R R_ tr2 .

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS B1358 113 as an FR II radio galaxy in Abell 1836, under-luminous for its morphology, with a massive weakly active black hole and LINER nucleus.

quotes.quote: Despite its clear FR II-like large-scale radio morphology, \ is not particularly bright at radio frequencies. The total 1.4 GHz radio flux density of its extended structure, 1.87 Jy (see radio ), implies a spectral power of P_ 1.4 GHz 0.6 10^ 25 W Hz ^ 1 , which is below the Fanaroff-Riley dividing line. Additionally, the host of \ is a particularly bright galaxy, with a R band absolute magnitude of M_ R 23.0 see converted using the 158 Mpc distance to the source adopted here owe89,lai03 .

gmw_00000317.name: Abell 3565-BCG
gmw_00000317.overview: Abell 3565-BCG is a giant elliptical brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:1903.03198, arXiv:2403.03974] that hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:astro-ph/0610702] and a warped, disk-like molecular gas structure [arXiv:1908.09229, arXiv:2210.14922]. It contains the active radio source PKS 1333-33 [arXiv:2111.02430, arXiv:2403.03974], which features symmetric, knotty jets [arXiv:astro-ph/0009116, arXiv:1903.03198] and extended radio lobes [arXiv:1903.03198, arXiv:2111.02430].

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1333-33 as a nearby southern active radio galaxy whose outer lobes may accelerate protons, highlighting its core, jets, extended lobes, and eastern-lobe synchrotron working surface.

quotes.quote: The present letter is devoted to CR production in the outer region of the nearby southern active galaxy PKS 1333-33 and the spectral modifications arisen from their propagation through intergalactic space. The radio galaxy PKS 1333-33 is made up of a core, two symmetric jets, and two extended radio lobes.

An interesting feature of PKS 1333-33 is an intense region of synchrotron emission localized at the outer edge of the eastern lobe. This region can be considered as a ``working surface'' formed by the deceleration of the jet. This interpretation is supported by VLA polarimetric observations, which show a change in the field orientation from parallel to perpendicular to the jet axis .

This change is probably due to the rearrangement of the field lines in the post-shock region. The degree of linear polarization in the eastern lobe is in the range 20 40 . The synchrotron parameters estimated for this region are: the minimum energy density ( _ min = 2.1 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 3 ), the minimum magnetic field ( B_ min = 1.5 10^ 6 G), the minimum total pressure ( P_ min = 1.1 10^ 13 dyne cm ^ 2 ), and the degree of linear polarization ( m = 20-40 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4296 as CO-faint, with low-significance disk-like molecular emission, no clear central high-velocity emission, and stellar rotation roughly co-rotating with CO.

quotes.quote: We constructed velocity profiles for NGC 4374 (Figure  ngc4374_pvd_velchan ) and IC 4296 (Figure  ic4296_pvd_velchan ) by integrating the image cube flux densities in each 40 km s ^ 1 channel over their 2 wide optical dust disks (described by the major/minor axes in Table  tbl-obspars ). The resulting profiles are very different from the symmetric, double-horned shapes that we observe for the CO-bright galaxies. We do detect CO(2 1) emission, but at much lower ( 10 and 5 ) significance than the CO-bright subsample. Their PVDs, extracted along the central dust disk major axes, show faint evidence of disk-like rotation. In both galaxies, careful inspection of the image cubes reveals regions of statistically significant ( 3 in a 40 km s ^ 1 channel) emission across many channels that spatially coincide with the optically thick dust absorption and that show an outline of the full disk rotation.

The IC 4296 PVD and approximate velocity maps are consistent with no central high-velocity emission above the background level (although it cannot be ruled out due to the coarse, 0 5 resolution); in NGC 4374 , however, we see hints of a velocity upturn within r_ g with emission spanning at least 700 km s ^ 1 directly across the nucleus. IFU data for NGC 4374 show almost no evidence for stellar rotation ems11 , and the galaxy is expected to be (mildly) triaxial; stellar photometric axes of this slow rotator are misaligned by nearly 90 with respect to the inner dust disk major axis, the ionized gas kinematics (e.g., wal10 ), and now seemingly also the cold molecular gas rotation. Long-slit spectroscopy of IC 4296 suggests more significant ( 100 km s ^ 1 ) stellar rotation that is roughly consistent with the stellar photometric axes (PA 60-70 ; efs80 , kil86 ) and appears to co-rotate to within about 20 with the faint CO-emission (in Figure  ic4296_pvd_velchan ).

sources.summary: The paper compares IC 4296 with Galactic-center filaments, describing its elliptical host, radio filaments, jet connection, spectral behavior, and possible particle injection.

quotes.quote: IC 4296 is a low-luminosity elliptical galaxy, in the cluster A3566 at a distance of 50 Mpc condon21 . A sensitive radio image of this galaxy shows a remarkable group of three filaments with angular spacing of 20'' (called threads in ) that appear to converge to a point. The projected lengths and widths of the filaments are 50 and 2 kpc, respectively, with surface brightness of 0.1 mJy per 7.6'' beam condon21 . This group of filaments lies to the south of the western radio jet where the jet shows a wiggle, thought to be due to Kelvin-Helmholtz (KH) instability condon21 .

For example, the filaments of radio galaxy IC 4296 are thought to originate where helical KH instability disrupts the flow of the radio jet followed by the relativistic particles feeding the filaments condon21 . One puzzle is why the spectrum flattens instead of steepening moving away from the presumed injection point at the end of the cometary structure's tail towards the convergence point of the filaments. One possible explanation for this is that the injected particles' pitch angle would tend to increase to conserve the magnetic moment p_ /B as they spiral along the converging field lines.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1333 33 as a large transition-power radio galaxy with a self-absorbed nucleus, symmetric knotty jets, hard nuclear X-rays, and marginal parsec-scale resolution.

quotes.quote: PKS 1333 33 (IC 4296) PKS 1333 33 is a very large radio galaxy (Killeen et al. 1986), with radio power typical of transition objects between FRI and FRII sources (see Table 1). Its radio morphology reflects the radio power, and it is also intermediate between these two classes. The source is characterised by an arcsecond self-absorbed nucleus, which peaks at 15 GHz, and by two symmetric knotty jets which culminate in two ``warm'' spots at a distance of 12^ from the core, i.e. 250 kpc. The associated optical counterpart is a nearby elliptical galaxy, with a featureless optical spectrum. X--ray observations carried out with ASCA and BeppoSAX (Fiore et al. in preparation), revealed the existence of a hard X--ray excess over the thermal stellar component, most likely of nuclear origin, which led them to propose that a low efficiency accretion flow is hosted in the nuclear region of the galaxy.

PKS1333 33 is almost unresolved at the resolution and sensitivity of the present 2.29 GHz observations. As for PKS 1318 43, due to sensitivity limits, the very noisy data points on the longest baselines forced us to taper the u-v coverage in order to get a reliable image, thus degrading the FWHM.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes IC 4296 as a giant elliptical radio galaxy whose AGN jets, radio lobes, X-ray cavity, and cooling atmosphere trace powerful feedback into Abell 3565's intracluster medium.

quotes.quote: The powerful AGN jets penetrating the innermost hot galactic atmosphere and depositing most of their energy out at radii 230 kpc make the giant elliptical galaxy IC 4296, in the central region of the galaxy cluster Abell 3565, a source of high interest. Its radio counterpart, PKS 1333-33, has attracted the attention of astronomers for over 30 years.

The new high-resolution VLA data in A configuration reveal the bright central region of IC4296 and two almost symmetrical knotty radio jets extending far beyond the host galaxy. With the VLA D configuration data, we confirm the presence of well-defined radio lobes first reported by killeen1986I . The lobes, without significant hot spots, have large diameters of 160 kpc at radii 230 kpc and are undetected in the higher resolution A configuration VLA images. The XMM-Newton data reveal the presence of an X-ray cavity associated with one of the large radio lobes indicating a remarkable total enthalpy 7 10^ 59 erg .

sources.summary: The paper finds IC 4296 has a CO rotation pattern best reproduced with a warp, possible minor-axis non-circular motions, lopsided disc edges, and radio-core absorption.

quotes.quote: IC 4296 Although the basic model described in Section sec:gas_distrib_kinem provides a reasonable representation of the observations, the CO rotation pattern of IC 4296 is well reproduced only when accounting for the presence of a warp (Fig. fig:IC4296_mom1 and fig:IC4296_mom1_mod ). Specifically, we let the kinematic position angle vary linearly in the radial direction along the disc, finding a best-fitting range of 240 -- 220 degrees (Table tab:first_model ). Nevertheless, low level residuals (slightly larger than the channel width =40 km s ^ 1 ) are still visible along the disc minor axis (Fig. fig:IC4296_mom1_res ), possibly suggesting the presence of non-circular motions.

It is hard, however, to establish whether such motions are truly present or not, given the resolution of the current dataset (Table tab:summary ). The gas distribution is well reproduced by this axisymmetric model, even though the gas disc is lopsided at its edges (Fig. fig:IC4296_mom1 ). We note that an absorption feature against the radio core is detected in this source (see Paper I), giving rise to the hole in the gas distribution visible in the velocity curve (Fig. fig:IC4296_PVD ).

sources.summary: IC 4296 is analyzed as a molecular absorber with a small disc-like gas structure whose absorption matches clouds within the galaxy disc.

quotes.quote: IC 4296 has a molecular mass of 2.3 0.2 10^ 7 M _ in a disc-like structure approximately 0.8 kpc wide and with a weak velocity gradient find a molecular mass of 2.0 0.2 10^ 7 M _ Ruffa2019 . The angular resolution of the observation is poor, so only an upper limit of 0.2 kpc can be placed on the disc's width. Emission with velocities matched to the absorption is spread across most of the disc-like structure, except at its extremities.

The properties of the molecular absorption are entirely consistent with it being due to clouds within the disc of the galaxy. The covering factor of the continuum is 170 M _ /pc ^ 2 , similar to the actual column density of the absorption along the line of sight of 290 M _ /pc ^ 2 . The absorption also has a narrow velocity dispersion (for the deepest line, = 4 km/s, FWHM = 9.4 km/s) and the absorbing clouds lie at the peak of the molecular emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 4296 as the closest NGC 4261 analog, with a steep entropy profile, central t_cool/t_ff near 10, powerful radio source, and no extended multiphase gas beyond 2 kpc.

quotes.quote: We find that one of them, IC 4296, exhibits properties similar to NGC 4261, including the presence of only single phase gas outside of 2 kpc and a similar central velocity dispersion. We compare the properties of NGC 4261 and IC 4296 to hydrodynamic simulations of AGN feedback fueled by precipitation.

Our analysis of the entropy profiles for a sample of nearby early-type galaxies with powerful radio sources shows that at least one other galaxy (IC 4296) is like NGC 4261 in having a powerful AGN and t_ cool /t_ ff 10 at < 1 kpc. While the spatial resolution of the X-ray data for IC 4296 is not as good as for NGC 4261, both of their entropy profiles appear to be single power laws, and neither has extended multiphase gas greater than 2 kpc from their nuclei. To be certain of their similarity, we will need additional Chandra observations of IC 4296 to match the data quality of NGC 4261.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4296 as an FR I source with symmetric parsec-scale double-sided morphology, a spectral-index feature, and nearly stationary symmetric jet components during monitoring.

quotes.quote: 1333 337 (IC 4296) This FR I source shows a symmetric double-sided morphology on parsec scales (see Fig. fig:wallpaper ), extending in a direction consistent with its large scale structure 1986ApJ...302..306K . The spectral index map, presented in Fig. 1333_spix , shows an unresolved feature with a spectral index 0.8 .

1333 337 We find that the two symmetric jet components (J1 and CJ1) show almost no motion during our monitoring period. Although we do find a non-zero separation speed for component CJ1, the uncertainty on this value is higher than 50 . In Fig. beta_theta , we adopt the only non-zero apparent speed value as the central estimate, and use its uncertainty to define the minimum and maximum estimates.

sources.summary: The paper includes IC 4296 among FR I LERGs with resolved CO discs, using archival 5 GHz data for its faint jets and HST imaging for an unambiguous jet-disc angle.

quotes.quote: Five of the six sources detected in ^ 12 CO(2-1) were found to satisfy these criteria: IC 1531, NGC 3100, NGC 3557, IC 4296 and NGC 7075 (NGC 612 was excluded as we found no evidence for small-scale jet structure). Four of these sources show straight FR I jet structures, allowing us to estimate jet inclinations and, in turn, intrinsic jet--CO disc orientations as described in Section sec:method .

In IC 4296, emission from the brightest knots of the North-West jet is only barely visible in our 10-GHz observations (Fig. fig:ic4296_VLA ) and emission from the South-East jet is undetected. We therefore used deep high-resolution archival VLA data at 5 GHz (whose re-imaged version is presented in Paper I and Figure fig:ic4296_boxes ) to estimate the jet sidedness ratio.

For NGC 3557, IC 4296, NGC 383 and NGC 3665 the available archival Hubble Space Telescope (HST) optical imaging allows us to identify the side of the CO disc pointing towards the observer unequivocally (Paper I; Onishi17,North19 ) and we can derive unique values of _ dj .

sources.summary: The paper presents MeerKAT imaging of IC 4296 as a nearby elliptical radio galaxy with a 510 kpc FR I source, highlighting threads, ribbons, rings, jets, and lobes.

quotes.quote: The nearby elliptical galaxy IC 4296 has produced a large (510 kpc) low-luminosity radio source with typical FR i core/jet/lobe morphology. The unprecedented combination of brightness sensitivity, dynamic range, and angular resolution of a new 1.28 GHz MeerKAT continuum image reveals striking new morphological features which we call threads, ribbons, and rings. The threads are faint narrow emission features originating where helical Kelvin-Helmholtz instabilities disrupt the main radio jets. The ribbons are smooth regions between the jets and the lobes, and they appear to be relics of jets powered by earlier activity that have since come into pressure equilibrium. Vortex rings in the outer portions of the lobes and their backflows indicate that the straight outer jets and ribbons are inclined by i =60^ 5^ from the line-of-sight, in agreement with photometric, geometric, and gas-dynamical estimates of inclination angles near the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper observes Abell 3565-BCG with HST spectroscopy and imaging, modeling its gas disk to determine a 1.3e9 solar-mass black hole and stellar mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: We observed a sample of three Brightest Cluster Galaxies (BCGs), Abell 1836-BCG, Abell 2052-BCG, and Abell 3565-BCG, with the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) and the Imaging Spectrograph (STIS) on board the Space Telescope. For each target galaxy we obtained high-resolution spectroscopy of the H and [ N II ] 6583 emission lines at three slit positions, to measure the central ionized-gas kinematics. ACS images in three different filters (F435W, F625W, and FR656N) have been used to determine the optical depth of the dust, stellar mass distribution near the nucleus, and intensity map.
For the SBHs of Abell 1836-BCG and Abell 3565-BCG, we derived M_ =4.8^ +0.8 _ 0.7 10^9 M _ and M_ =1.3^ +0.3 _ 0.4 10^9 M _ at 1 confidence level, respectively.
For Abell 3565-BCG we determine M_ =1.3^ +0.3 _ 0.4 10^9 M _ , with i=50^ 1^ and M/L_I =9.0 0.8 M/L _I _ ( 1 confidence level).

sources.summary: The paper records IC 4296's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: IC 4296: McConnellMa2013 and KormendyHo2013 consider the galaxy a core elliptical; we accept their classification, although strong nuclear dust makes it difficult to determine its core properties see discussion in Rusli2013b . We measure the profile from I- band FORS images that we calibrate to Cousins I using the photometry of Prugniel1998 and finally to the B band using the mean (B-I)=2.41 color derived from Goudfrooij1994 and A_B=0.265 . From this profile we measure R_e=68.1'' , in agreement with R_e=70.9'' Laesker2014 'best fit', but disagreeing with their 'classical fit' ( R_e=28.9'' ) or the value reported by 32.3'' Sani2011 . The M/L (from the model with dark matter and already corrected for G.E.) is scaled to the distance of KormendyHo2013 .

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4296 as an Abell 3565 BCG hosting PKS 1333-33, with a nuclear dust disk, 77 kpc double-sided jet, warped CO disk, and core absorption.

quotes.quote: IC 4296 Galaxy and environment IC 4296 is an elliptical galaxy and the brightest cluster galaxy of Abell 3565. It hosts the FRI radio source PKS 1333-33, contains a nuclear dust disk and has a large-scale, double-sided jet that extends up to 77 kpc from the nucleus Lauer2005, Burke2009 . The inner jet is unresolved at 230 GHz but faint emission is detected at a distance of 950 pc North-West of the nucleus Ruffa2019 . Molecular emission IC 4296 has disky CO(2-1) emission with a mass of 2.3 0.2 10^ 7 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 4296 as an example where B-, V-, and R-band three-component photometric models produce good residuals and consistent structural parameters.

quotes.quote: Figure 3 shows an example for IC 4296. From the upper panel, we can see that the residuals are very good in all three filters. And judged by the surface brightness profiles and key parameters for the three components, the models in all three bands are indeed very consistent.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4296 as a giant elliptical CDG hosting PKS 1333-33, with central emission, 150 kpc jets, knots, wiggles, and large radio lobes.

quotes.quote: IC 4296* IC 4296 is a giant elliptical galaxy and the CDG of Abell 3565. The radio emission identified with the radio source PKS 1333-33, extends well beyond the host galaxy IC 4296. Our VLA A and D configuration data (Figure fig:chandra_vla_a b) show a unresolved central radio source and extended radio emission in the form of two well-collimated (within the first 10 kpc from the core) radio jets expanding up to 150 kpc on both sides with many knots and wiggles and terminating in large radio lobes with a radius of 60-80 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper reports IC4296 as underpredicted by 4-7 orders of magnitude, groups it with elliptical radio galaxies, and finds the adjusted model within about one order.

quotes.quote: Sources that were under-predicted 4-7 orders of magnitude are: NGC1291, NGC1316, NGC1553, NGC4261, NGC4438, NGC4594, NGC4697 and IC4296.

NGC1291 is an early type spiral, NGC1316 is disturbed early type elliptical, NGC 1553 has a spiral feature passing threw a center, NGC 4261 core is surrounded by a disk of gas and dust, NGC4438 has an outflow bubbles which also may indicate angular momentum to dominate the accretion flow, NGC4594 is a spiral galaxy, the last two sourced NGC 4697 and IC4296 are elliptical radio galaxies.

Other sources (NGC1291, NGC1316, NGC4438, NGC4472, M87, NGC4594, NGC4697, IC1459, IC4296) differ from their observational counterparts of about 1 order of magnitude, lots of them even less than order of magnitude (see Table 1.)

gmw_00000318.name: XMMSL1J063045.9-603110
gmw_00000318.overview: XMMSL1J063045.9-603110 is a candidate host for a tidal disruption event [arXiv:1605.06133]. The host is an extremely small galaxy, possibly as faint as a dwarf spheroidal or a bright globular cluster [arXiv:1605.06133].

sources.summary: The paper presents XMMSL1J063045.9-603110 as a tidal-disruption candidate likely associated with an extremely small host galaxy, possibly as faint as dwarf spheroidals or bright globular clusters.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: RA=06:30:45.9, DEC=-60:31:10 ( 8'' error circle, 1 confidence level)

quotes.quote: In this paper we discuss XMMSL1J063045.9-603110 as a candidate object for a tidal disruption event. The source has recently been detected to be bright in the soft X-rays during an XMM-Newton slew and later showed an X-ray flux decay by a factor of about 10 in twenty days. We analyse XMM-Newton and Swift data. XMMSL1J063045.9-603110 shows several features typical of tidal disruption events: the X-ray spectrum shows the characteristics of a spectrum arising from a thermal accretion disc, the flux decay follows a t^ 5/3 law, and the flux variation is > 350 . Optical observations testify that XMMSL1J063045.9-603110 is probably associated with an extremely small galaxy or even a globular cluster, which suggests that intermediate-mass black holes are located in the cores of (at least) some of them.

The problem for XMMSL1J063045 is that no spectra deep enough of its dim host galaxy are currently available in which absorption lines might be identified to evaluate z . Using Eq. dMBH to estimate the source luminosity distance d_ pc (in parsec) from a BH of mass 10^4-10^6 M_ and considering the relation between the absolute magnitude ( M_ V ) and the apparent magnitude ( m_ V ), the dim host ends up with M_ V 5.7 10.7 . When L_ peak is assumed to be ten times the Eddington luminosity (which is very high), M_ V lies in the range 8.2 13.2 .

gmw_00000319.name: NGC 404
gmw_00000319.overview: NGC 404 is a nearby dwarf lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1001.4087, arXiv:1204.3089] hosting a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1002.0824, arXiv:1006.4893] and a central intermediate-mass black hole [arXiv:2109.12129, arXiv:1104.0981]. The galaxy features a complex circumnuclear dust distribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0411148], an extended neutral hydrogen shell [arXiv:1001.0234, arXiv:1105.4809], and a variable nuclear ultraviolet source [arXiv:1001.2924].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 404 as an S0 galaxy with a central IMBH, two nuclear star clusters, low rotation, and a stable analog with central density near 10^5 solar masses per cubic parsec.

quotes.quote: NGC 404 NGC 404 is S0 type galaxy which is considered to possess a central IMBH and two NSCs seth10,ngu17 . It has a mean velocity dispersion of about 35 km/s which rises to 40 km/s towards the center, hinting at the presence of an IMBH. The galaxy's mean rotational velocity is only about 8 km/s resulting in a low v/ 0.2   . ngu17 found the mass of the central IMBH to be 7 10^4 M_ having an influence radius of about 0.35 pc.

The total stellar mass is reported to be 8.6 10^8 M_ . Some key parameters of NSCs and bulge reported in ngu17 are presented in table tab:ngc404param . Shape parameters for this galaxy model are b/a 0.95 and /a 0.85 as well. Figure fig:ngc404multi shows our initial model and its stability for 20 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 404 as a nearby LINER with complex circumnuclear dust and gas, resolved stars, irregular dust clumps, and filamentary ionized gas likely affected by starburst activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 404 --- Fig. 1(b) : This extensively studied LINER galaxy, believed to be located just outside the Local Group, possesses the smallest stellar velocity dispersion in our sample (54 km sec ^ 1 ). Its distance remains a matter of debate - typical estimates have ranged from 1 Mpc (Baars Wendker 1976) to 10 Mpc (Wiklind Henkel 1990), but recent measurements from surface brightness fluctuations (Jensen et al. 2003) and color-magnitude diagrams (Tikhonov, Galazutdinova, Aparicio 2003) place it at a distance of 3.0-3.5 Mpc. We adopt the lower value of 3 Mpc. Previous HST images of NGC 404 in visible broad and narrow bands have been presented in Pogge et al. (2000), Tikhonov, Galazutdinova, Aparicio (2003) and Martini et al. (2003). The greater depth of our ACS images reveals more detail in the galaxy's circumnuclear regions.

As was found in the WFPC2 work, the dust and gas distributions are complex. Three distinct, irregular clumps of dust are found in the eastern half of the galaxy out to a radius of 50 pc ( 3 5 ) at PAs of 50 , 120 , and 180 . The overall orientation of the ionized gas is along the east-west axis.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC404 as a nearby low-luminosity lenticular with velocity inversions, a core co-rotating with its H I shell, multiple stellar populations, and evidence for merger-driven morphological transition.

quotes.quote: NGC404 is a nearly face-on nearby low-luminosity lenticular galaxy. Probing its characteristics provides a wealth of information on the details of possible evolution processes of dS0 galaxies which may not be possible in other, more distant objects. We study the internal kinematics and the spatial distribution of the star formation history in NGC404.

NGC404 clearly shows two radial velocity inversions along its major axis. The kinematically decoupled core rotates in the same direction as the neutral hydrogen shell that surrounds the galaxy. We resolved the star formation history in the core of the galaxy ino 4 events: A very young ( < 150 Myr, and [ Fe/H ] = 0.4) component with constant on-going star formation, a second young (430 Myr) component with [ Fe/H ] = 0.1, an intermediate population (1.7 Gyr) which has [ Fe/H ] = -0.05 and, finally, an old (12 Gyr) component with [ Fe/H ] = -1.26.
We conclude that NGC404 had a spiral morphology about 1 Gyr ago and that one or many merger events has triggered a morphological transition.

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC 404 as the nearest S0 galaxy, describing its total mass, LINER nucleus, and multi-component nuclear star cluster.

quotes.quote: We now focus on the NSC formation and possible intermediate mass BH in NGC 404, the nearest S0 galaxy (D=3.1 Mpc). The galaxy is nearly face-on and has a total mass of about 10^9 M _ and M_V = -17.35 . NGC 404 also hosts the nearest LINER nucleus, although whether this observed activity is actually the result of an accreting BH is a matter of some contention.

Nuclear Star Cluster The surface brightness profile of the inner part of NGC 404 (see left panel of Fig. aseth_nscfig ) can be decomposed into three distinct components each with distinct kinematics and stellar populations. The bulge of the galaxy has a mass of 9 10^8 M _ and dominates the light profile outside the central arcsecond (15 pc). Optical spectra of the stellar populations of this component show that it is dominated by old stars with ages > 5 Gyr. The nuclear star cluster dominates the light in the central arcsecond and has a half-light radius of 10 pc and a dynamical mass of 10^7 M _ , larger than expected from NSC scaling relations.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes GMOS IFU data for NGC 404, describing its LINER nature, old central stellar mass, past star-formation episodes, and possible AGN or shock ionization.

quotes.quote: NGC 404, at a distance of 3.4 Mpc, is the nearest S0 galaxy. This galaxy harbors a LINER; however, since the spectrum does not show a broad H emission, it is not certain that this LINER is a low luminosity AGN and its nature is still an open question. HST observations show the existence of stellar populations with an age of 3 10^ 8 years in the galactic bulge and with an age of 6-15 10^ 9 years in the galactic disk. In this work, we present an analysis of the data cube of NGC 404 obtained with the IFU (Integral Field Unity) of the GMOS (Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph) on the Gemini North telescope.

By using Starlight, we determined the flux and mass fractions corresponding to the stellar populations used in the spectral synthesis of the central region of NGC 404. In Figure 1 we can see that most of the stellar mass in the central region of this galaxy is due to stars with ages higher than 10^ 10 years and with medium and high metallicities. Therefore, photoionization models involving young stars cannot explain the emission spectrum of this galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 404 is described as a nearby dwarf lenticular galaxy with compact nuclear radio emission, most plausibly from an accreting intermediate-mass black hole rather than stellar processes.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The 5 GHz emission is centered at _ J2000  = 01 ^ h 09 ^ m 27.00 ^ s and _ J2000  = +35 43 04.91

quotes.quote: We present the results of deep, high-resolution, 5 GHz Expanded Very Large Array (EVLA) observations of the nearby, dwarf lenticular galaxy and intermediate mass black hole candidate ( M_ BH    4.5   10 ^5  M _ ), NGC 404. For the first time, radio emission at frequencies above 1.4 GHz has been detected in this galaxy. We found a modestly resolved source in the NGC 404 nucleus with a total radio luminosity of 7.6   0.7   10 ^ 17  W Hz ^ 1 at 5 GHz and a spectral index from 5 to 7.45 GHz of  =  0.88   0.30.

NGC 404 is only the third central intermediate mass black hole candidate detected in the radio regime with subarcsecond resolution. The position of the radio source is consistent with the optical center of the galaxy and the location of a known, hard X-ray point source ( L_ X    1.2   10 ^ 37  erg s ^ 1 ). The faint radio and X-ray emission could conceivably be produced by an X-ray binary, star formation, a supernova remnant or a low-luminosity AGN powered by an intermediate mass black hole. In light of our new EVLA observations, we find that the most likely scenario is an accreting intermediate mass black hole, with other explanations incompatible with the observed X-ray and/or radio luminosities or statistically unlikely.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 404’s LINER nucleus with near-infrared IFS, finding rotating thermal H2, diffuse high-velocity FeII, and shock-excited LINER emission likely tied to a supernova remnant.

quotes.quote: We present high spatial resolution, integral field spectrograph (IFS) observations of the nearby LINER (low ionization nuclear emission line region) galaxy NGC 404 at 1.25 m (J band) and 2.2 m (K band) near-infrared wavelengths. Although NGC 404 is thought to host an intermediate mass black hole at its center, it has been unclear whether accretion onto the black hole or another mechanism such as shock excitation drives its LINER emission at optical/near-infrared wavelengths. We use the OSIRIS IFS at Keck Observatory behind laser guide star adaptive optics to map the strength and kinematics of [ FeII ] , H _2 , and hydrogen recombination lines at spatial resolutions of 1 pc across the central 30 pc of the galaxy. The H _2 gas is in a central rotating disk and ratios of multiple H _2 lines indicate that the molecular gas is thermally excited, with some contribution from UV fluorescence. The [ FeII ] emission is more extended and diffuse than the molecular gas and has a different kinematic structure that reaches higher velocities/dispersions.

Finally, we find regions across the nucleus of NGC 404 with [ FeII ] /Pa line ratios up to 6.5, 2.5 times higher than the ratio measured from spatially-integrated spectra. From these high line ratios, we conclude that shocks are the dominate physical mechanism exciting NGC 404's LINER emission and argue that a possible source of this shock excitation is a supernova remnant.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 404 as a nearby isolated lenticular with an HI shell and UV-bright ring, grouping it with gas-bearing blue-sequence E/S0 galaxies.

quotes.quote: The nearby lenticular galaxy NGC 404 with M_B=-16 6 , which was found to host an extended HI shell DelRio2004 and ultraviolet-bright ring of young stars Thilker2009 , is a typical example.

The NGC 404, NGC 855, and NGC 4460 galaxies differ appreciably in luminosity, but have almost similar hydrogen masses 1 10^8M_ . Specific star-formation rate per unit luminosity differs little among these galaxies. The parameter P^*_K is only slightly smaller than zero for all three galaxies, and this indicates that the galaxies are capable of reproducing most of their stellar mass during cosmological time T_0 provided that star formation is at the current level. At the same time, the available gas reserves in these galaxies can maintain the current rate of star formation only for a short time scale ranging from 1/40 (NGC 4460) to 1/6 (NGC 404) of the age of the Universe.
All these properties are similar with the Local Volume isolated E/S0 galaxies described above, therefore NGC 4460, NGC 404, and NGC 855 seem to be belonging to the population of blue-sequence E/S0s.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 404's nucleus as UV/X-ray resolved, variable, and likely containing both a compact star-forming region and a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 404. -- A UV spectrum of the nucleus of this galaxy shows prominent absorption features from hot stars maoz98 , indicating that they make a significant contribution to the light at these wavelengths. The X-ray spectrum is very soft see the discussion in eracleous02 , with =2.5 , which is uncharacteristic of AGNs cf, nandra97 , although an AGN cannot be ruled out based on this observation. The nuclear source is resolved at both UV and X-ray wavelengths and it has a ``blow-out'' morphology maoz95,eracleous02 . On the other hand, the compact nucleus of the UV source appears to be variable by a factor of approximately two on a time scale of approximately a decade. In addition, the depths of the absorption lines appear to be shallower than the those found in the spectra of hot stars maoz05,maoz07 .
All of these properties suggest that the nucleus of NGC 404 harbors a compact star-forming region as well as a low-luminosity AGN.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLA and ALMA observations of NGC 404, resolving nuclear radio emission and arguing that a confined AGN jet or outflow likely powers the central radio source.

quotes.quote: The nearby dwarf galaxy NGC 404 harbors a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus (AGN) powered by the lowest-mass (< 150,000 M _ ) central massive black hole (MBH) with a dynamical mass constraint currently known, thus providing a rare low-redshift analog to the MBH ``seeds'' that formed in the early Universe. Here, we present new imaging of the nucleus of NGC 404 at 12--18 GHz with the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) and observations of the CO(2--1) line with the Atacama Large Millimeter/Submillimeter Array (ALMA). For the first time, we have successfully resolved the nuclear radio emission, revealing a centrally peaked, extended source spanning 17 pc.

The peak of the resolved radio source coincides with the dynamical center of NGC 404, the center of a rotating disk of molecular gas, and the position of a compact, hard X-ray source. We also present evidence for shocks in the NGC 404 nucleus from archival narrowband HST imaging, Chandra X-ray data, and Spitzer mid-infrared spectroscopy, and discuss possible origins for the shock excitation. Given the morphology, location, and steep spectral index of the resolved radio source, as well as constraints on nuclear star formation from the ALMA CO(2--1) data, we find the most likely scenario for the origin of the radio source in the center of NGC 404 to be a radio outflow associated with a confined jet driven by the active nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 404 as a nearby dwarf galaxy with a confined nuclear radio jet, low-mass low-Eddington SMBH, shock-excited gas, and multiwavelength AGN feedback signatures.

quotes.quote: NGC 404: A Case Study In Figure fig:radio_alma_hst , we illustrate the multiwavelength nuclear properties of the nearby dwarf galaxy NGC 404, which harbors a nuclear radio jet with an extent of 10 pc characterized by a steep radio spectral index from 1 to 18 GHz ( 1 ; nyland+17 ). The spatial coincidence of the radio source, hard X-ray source, dynamical center of NGC 404, and the rotating circumnuclear CO(2--1) disk are most consistent with a confined radio jet launched by the central low-mass ( M_ BH <10^5 M_ from stellar dynamical modeling; nguyen+17 ), low-Eddington-ratio ( L_ bol /L_ Edd 10^ 6 ; nyland+12 ) SMBH that has been disrupted by the ambient interstellar medium (ISM). Multiwavelength evidence for shock excitation supports the possibility of an interaction between the confined jet and the ISM.

This includes the detection of extended H and soft X-ray emission from HST and Chandra , respectively, as well as strong [ Fe II ] at 26 m and rotational H _2 emission in archival Spitzer spectroscopic data nyland+17 . The nucleus of NGC 404 therefore offers a unique local laboratory for directly constraining the energetic impact on the ambient ISM by a jetted AGN hosted by a dwarf galaxy. However, the identification of additional jetted AGNs in low-mass galaxies will ultimately be needed to place NGC 404 in the broader context of galaxy evolution.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep Chandra observations of NGC 404, separating hard nuclear and soft extended emission and favoring an intermediate-mass black hole powering a very weak AGN.

quotes.quote: The long exposure and excellent spatial resolution of Chandra has enabled us to critically examine the nuclear environment of NGC 404, which is known to host a nuclear star cluster and potentially an intermediate-mass black hole (on the order of a few times 10^5 ). We find two distinct X-ray sources: a hard, central point source coincident with the optical and radio centers of the galaxy, and a soft extended region that is coincident with areas of high H emission and likely recent star formation. When we fit the 0.3-8 keV spectra of each region separately, we find the hard nuclear point source to be dominated by a power law ( = 1.88), while the soft off-nuclear region is best fit by a thermal plasma model ( kT = 0.67 keV). We therefore find evidence for both a power law component and hot gas in the nuclear region of NGC 404.

We compare our results to the observed relationships between power law photon index and Eddington ratio for both X-ray binaries and low luminosity active galaxies and find NGC 404 to be consistent with other low luminosity active galaxies. We therefore favor the conclusion that NGC 404 harbors an intermediate-mass black hole accreting at a very low level.

The optical center of the NGC 404 nucleus centered on the S3 chip and detected at _ J2000 = 01 ^ h 09 ^ m 26. ^ s 99 and _ J2000 = +35 ^ 43 05. ^ 1 (with a positional uncertainty of 0. ^ 02) in good agreement (within 0. ^ 4) with the position reported in SIMBAD See http://simbad.u-strasbg.fr/. (Cotton et al.

sources.summary: NGC 404 is described as a nearby isolated face-on dS0 with ongoing star formation, dust, an H i ring, LINER emission, and complex star-formation history.

quotes.quote: NGC 404 A nearly face-on dS0 galaxy, placed 3.4 Mpc e.g. tonry2001 from the Local Group and fairly isolated. It displays ongoing star formation ho1993 and a dust lane with complex structure within 5 arcsec from the centre e.g. gallagher1986 . delrio2004 found a ring of neutral hydrogen (H i ) ( M_ HI = 1.5 10^8 M_ ) surrounding the stellar disc. NGC 404 has also a low-ionisation nuclear emission line region LINER pogge2000 . bouchard2010 performed spectroscopic studies and found a complex star-formation history (with more recent star-formation near the centre), nearly flat metallicity and age profiles within the inner 20 arcsec and decreasing increasing ages metallicities outside. Here, we used the same observations as in bouchard2010 . NGC 404 has a very peculiar kinematical structure with two inversions of the velocity gradient, and the innermost region ( < 3 arcsec or 45 pc) of the galaxy rotates in the same direction as the H i delrio2004,bouchard2010 .

sources.summary: NGC 404 is modeled as a nearby dwarf S0 with a weak central engine, a confined 10-pc jet, shocks, and possible localized AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: We present simulations of a confined jet with an extent of 10 pc analogous to the source residing in the nucleus of the nearby ( D=3.1  Mpc), dwarf S0 galaxy NGC 404 in Figure  fig:NGC404_sim . The central engine of this galaxy is weakly active and powered by a MBH with a mass upper limit from stellar dynamical modeling of M_ BH < 10^5  M _ nguyen+17 , making it the current record holder for the central MBH with the lowest dynamically-constrained mass. As mentioned in Section  sec:dwarf_galaxies , multiwavelength evidence for shocks in the center of NGC 404 associated with the confined jet raise the intriguing possibility of localized AGN feedback caused by the interaction between the jet and the nuclear molecular gas nyland+17 .

Figure  fig:NGC404_sim clearly indicates that the VLA cannot spatially resolve the morphology of a 10 pc jet at a distance of 10 Mpc in the A-configuration. However, the ngVLA is able to successfully image the extended structure of the 10-pc-scale jet given the same input model and observing frequency.

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 404 as a low-mass galaxy with fundamental-plane black hole evidence, tighter dynamical upper limits, and possible jet feedback.

quotes.quote: Another use of the fundamental plane to find evidence for a low-mass central black hole is in the low-mass galaxy NGC 404, where the plane yielded a mass estimate of log M/ = 6.4 1.1 nylandetal2012 . Newer observations may support an AGN interpretation of this system, although the evidence is not yet conclusive nylandetal2017 . On the other hand, dynamical studies of NGC 404 give a 3 upper limit of < 1.5 10^5 M_ nguyenetal2017 .
nylandetal2017 also identify possible evidence for feedback in the jet in NGC 404.

sources.summary: The survey notes that NGC 404 has a larger-disk-like double-horned H I profile, foreground Milky Way contamination near its systemic velocity, and low peak column densities.

quotes.quote: On the other end of the mass spectrum, a few galaxies (NGC 404 NGC 3741, DDO 190, Sextans A, DDO 181, NGC 3109, NGC 247) exhibit hints of the more familiar double-horned profile expected from larger spiral disks.
Some data cubes are contaminated by Galactic foreground emission, but only for NGC 404 is the Galactic \ close to the systemic velocity of the source.
Some galaxies, like KK 230, or NGC 404 have low columns with peak values of a few times 10^ 20 .

sources.summary: The paper estimates a black-hole mass for the nearby dwarf lenticular NGC 404 from its extinction-corrected H-band magnitude and compares it with stellar-dynamical limits.

quotes.quote: For the nearby dwarf lenticular galaxy NGC 404, the Galactic extinction corrected H band magnitude of 19.65 mag from Seth et al. \ (2010) becomes M_ K_s = -19.85 mag when using an H-K_s color of 0.2 mag (e.g. \ Balcells et al. \ 2007). Thought to house an AGN (e.g. \ Nyland et al. \ 2012, and references therein), the expected black hole mass in NGC 404 is M_ bh /M_ = 4.1 1.1 , using a magnitude error of 0.3 mag. For comparison, although restricted by assumptions about the inner stellar mass-to-light ratio, the stellar dynamical modeling from Seth et al. \ (2010) suggests that NGC 404 has a black hole mass less than 10^5 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 404 as a weak nuclear-black-hole candidate with low black-hole mass, young nuclear cluster, and optical evidence for A-star dominated light.

quotes.quote: NGC 404 (L2) While the hard X-ray point source at the center of NGC 404 is faint enough that it could be an X-ray binary, the spectral shape of the X-ray emission and the radio characteristics of this object suggest the presence of an accreting nuclear black hole Binder11,Nyland12 .
